taxonomy.label: Foundational Models & Representation Learning About Radar Signals
Pose Estimation & Skeletal Tracking & Human Motion

papers.title: Attention-Based Neural-Augmented Kalman Filter for Legged Robot State Estimation
papers.summary: Addresses the state estimation errors caused by foot slip in legged robots by proposing an Attention-Based Neural-Augmented Kalman Filter (AttenNKF). This method augments an Invariant Extended Kalman Filter (InEKF) with a neural compensator that uses an attention mechanism to infer slip-induced errors and apply post-update compensation. The compensator operates in a latent space to reduce sensitivity and ensure structured corrections, significantly improving performance under slip-prone conditions.

papers.title: RMD: Robust Modal Decomposition with Constrained Bandwidth
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of existing modal decomposition by mapping the signal into a phase-space trajectory-GRAM matrix to preserve intrinsic structure and incorporating bandwidth constraints to enhance noise resistance.

papers.title: A Data-centric Supervised Transfer Learning Framework for DOA Estimation with Array Imperfections
papers.summary: Addresses DOA estimation accuracy degradation caused by array imperfections through a supervised transfer learning framework that adapts models trained on ideal arrays to real-world conditions.

papers.title: NEAR: Neural Electromagnetic Array Response
papers.summary: Models electromagnetic array responses as continuous neural fields to improve DOA estimation by capturing complex antenna patterns and mutual coupling effects.

papers.title: Advancing Single-Snapshot DOA Estimation with Siamese Neural Networks for Sparse Linear Arrays
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
papers.summary: Achieves accurate single-snapshot DOA estimation for sparse linear arrays using Siamese neural networks that learn similarity metrics between spatial patterns.

papers.title: Advancing High-Resolution and Efficient Automotive Radar Imaging through Domain-Informed 1D Deep Learning
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
papers.summary: Enhances automotive radar imaging resolution and computational efficiency by incorporating radar signal processing domain knowledge into 1D convolutional neural networks.

papers.title: Model-Based Knowledge-Driven Learning Approach for Enhanced High-Resolution Automotive Radar Imaging
papers.summary: Combines model-based signal processing with deep unfolding networks to achieve super-resolution radar imaging while maintaining physical interpretability.

papers.title: Single-Frame MIMO Radar Velocity Vector Estimation via Multi-Bounce Scattering
papers.summary: Estimates full velocity vectors from single-frame MIMO radar data by exploiting multi-bounce scattering paths that provide additional geometric constraints.

papers.title: Doppler Former: Velocity Supervision of Raw Radar Data
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Introduces a novel Doppler supervision mechanism for raw radar data to enhance velocity estimation accuracy in challenging scenarios.

papers.title: Direction of Arrival Estimation with Virtual Antenna Array Using FMCW Radar Simulated Data
papers.summary: Addresses the underutilization of velocity information in raw radar data by introducing a plug-and-play 'Doppler Former' module designed to explicitly extract Doppler features and boost perception model performance.

papers.title: BFAR: improving radar odometry estimation using a bounded false alarm rate detector
papers.summary: Improves radar odometry accuracy by implementing a bounded false alarm rate detector that filters spurious detections while preserving valid targets.

papers.title: Antenna Failure Resilience: Deep Learning-Enabled Robust DOA Estimation with Single Snapshot Sparse Arrays
papers.publication: 58th Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of single-snapshot DOA estimation in sparse arrays, especially under antenna failure, by proposing a deep learning framework with a novel sparse signal augmentation layer.

papers.title: SDOA-Net: An Efficient Deep-Learning-Based DOA Estimation Network for Imperfect Array
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Addresses DOA estimation degradation in imperfect arrays by using a deep network that inputs raw signals, rather than covariance matrices, to generate a target-number-agnostic vector for spatial spectrum estimation.

papers.title: Unfolding Target Detection with State Space Model
papers.summary: Solves the poor performance and difficult tuning of classical CFAR detectors by "unfolding" the algorithm into a state space model, making its parameters learnable while retaining interpretability.

papers.title: A Two-Stage Multi-Layer Perceptron for High-Resolution DOA Estimation
papers.summary: Solves the poor resolution of existing deep learning DOA methods by using a two-stage MLP that first estimates a coarse grid and then fine-tunes the angle within that grid.

papers.title: Radar-STDA: A High-Performance Spatial-Temporal Denoising Autoencoder for Interference Mitigation of FMCW Radars
papers.summary: Tackles the inefficiency of existing deep learning-based radar interference mitigation by proposing a nano-sized, high-speed spatial-temporal denoising autoencoder that achieves high performance with minimal computational cost.

papers.title: End-to-End Trainable Deep Neural Network for Radar Interference Detection and Mitigation
papers.summary: Proposes an end-to-end deep learning framework that jointly detects and mitigates radar interference, outperforming traditional methods in both accuracy and speed.

papers.title: Experiments with mmWave Automotive Radar Test-bed
papers.publication: 53rd Asilomar Conference on Signals, Systems, and Computers
papers.summary: Details the creation of a lab-scale FMCW radar test-bed using a TI chipset and presents a new, large raw dataset collected with it, validating the platform's utility for ADAS research through preliminary object recognition results.

papers.title: Review of deep learning for SAR 3D imaging
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of traditional 3D SAR imaging (accuracy, efficiency, robustness) by systematically reviewing Deep Learning (DL) approaches that integrate neural networks with physical radar models. The review focuses on super-resolution and enhanced imaging techniques, while also outlining available datasets, current challenges (such as elevation disambiguation and generalization), and future trends in the field.

papers.title: Large Language Models Can Achieve Explainable and Training-Free One-shot HRRP ATR
papers.summary: Pioneers a training-free, explainable approach for one-shot radar target recognition by converting 1D HRRP signals into textual scattering center descriptions, allowing general-purpose LLMs to classify targets via in-context learning without parameter updates.

papers.title: Wave-Former: Through-Occlusion 3D Reconstruction via Wireless Shape Completion
papers.summary: Overcomes the sparsity and noise of mmWave signals for through-occlusion reconstruction by employing a physics-aware Transformer pipeline that bridges wireless signals with vision-based shape completion via entropy-guided surface selection, enabling robust generalization from synthetic training data.

papers.title: GeRaF: Neural Geometry Reconstruction from Radio Frequency Signals
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Addresses the unique challenges of lens-less, full-space RF signal propagation by introducing a physics-based volumetric rendering pipeline with a novel sampling and blending strategy, making neural 3D reconstruction feasible.

papers.title: A Single-Frequency Autofocusing Method Based on Conditional Diffusion Model for Millimeter-Wave Near-Range Imaging
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
papers.summary: Solves the defocusing problem of single-frequency imaging by using an encoder to convert the 3D defocused image into a 2D condition, which then guides a diffusion model to generate a high-quality, focused result.

papers.title: Millimeter Wave Inverse Pinhole Imaging
papers.summary: Enhances the angular resolution of static, compact radars by introducing a rotating physical 'inverse pinhole,' and demonstrates that drone propellers can naturally serve this function for high-resolution imaging while hovering.

papers.title: Unsupervised 3D SAR Imaging Network Based on Generative Adversary Learning
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
papers.summary: Achieves 3D SAR image reconstruction without paired training data by leveraging generative adversarial networks to learn imaging transformations in an unsupervised manner.

papers.title: RF4D:Neural Radar Fields for Novel View Synthesis in Outdoor Dynamic Scenes
papers.summary: Synthesizes novel radar views of dynamic outdoor scenes by representing 4D radar signals as continuous neural fields that encode spatial and temporal information.

papers.title: Millimeter-Wave SAR imaging of Sparse Trajectory via Untrained Complex-valued Neural Network
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
papers.summary: Reconstructs high-quality SAR images from sparse trajectory measurements using untrained complex-valued neural networks that exploit inherent signal structure without requiring pre-training.

papers.title: Novel Hybrid-Learning Algorithms for Improved Millimeter-Wave Imaging Systems
papers.summary: Proposes a suite of novel hybrid-learning algorithms that interleave signal processing and deep learning to achieve a superior balance of performance and generalizability across diverse mmWave imaging applications.

papers.title: High-resolution mmWave Imaging using Metasurface and Diffusion
papers.summary: Achieves high-resolution, static mmWave imaging by using an optimized metasurface to encode the scene and a diffusion model to reconstruct the image, replacing mechanical scanning and compressive sensing.

papers.title: IFNet: Deep Imaging and Focusing for Handheld SAR With Millimeter-Wave Signals
papers.summary: Solves severe handheld motion-induced phase errors in mmWave SAR by proposing a deep unfolding network that embeds signal processing priors into its iterative structure to achieve robust imaging without external trackers.

papers.title: IFNet: Imaging and Focusing Network for handheld mmWave Devices
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
papers.summary: Enables high-quality SAR imaging with handheld mmWave devices by introducing a deep learning framework that compensates for motion-induced phase errors and enhances image focusing.

papers.title: A high-resolution handheld millimeter-wave imaging system with phase error estimation and compensation
papers.summary: Proposes a handheld mmWave imaging system that estimates and compensates for phase errors caused by hand motion, achieving high-resolution imaging without external tracking.

papers.title: DART: Implicit Doppler Tomography for Radar Novel View Synthesis
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Proposes a NeRF-inspired method that uses a physics-based (reflectance and transmittance) rendering pipeline to synthesize realistic range-Doppler radar images from novel viewpoints.

papers.title: Towards large-scale single-shot millimeter-wave imaging for low-cost security inspection
papers.summary: Reduces antenna array cost by an order of magnitude by statistically ranking elements to create an optimal sparse layout, and then uses an untrained learning scheme for robust reconstruction from the sparse data.

papers.title: Fast-Fourier Time-Domain SAR Reconstruction for Millimeter-Wave FMCW 3-D Imaging
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
papers.summary: Accelerates 3D FMCW SAR imaging by two orders of magnitude by introducing a fast time-domain reconstruction algorithm (using FFTs/NUFFT) that leverages a novel two-step phase calibration.

papers.title: FMCW Inverse Circular Synthetic Aperture Radar Using a Fast Time-Domain Reconstruction
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
papers.summary: Accelerates inverse circular SAR imaging for real-time applications by adapting frequency-domain wavefront reconstruction into a fast time-domain algorithm and using an analytical PSF to speed up deconvolution.

papers.title: CoIR: Compressive Implicit Radar
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
papers.summary: Achieves data-agnostic, high-resolution sparse radar imaging by leveraging the implicit neural bias of a convolutional decoder as a structural prior, replacing the need for training data or traditional sparsity assumptions.

papers.title: Quantitative Investigation of Imaging Quality vs. Radar Position Errors in Millimeter-wave SAR
papers.summary: Experimentally quantifies how SAR imaging quality at 77 GHz degrades with increasing 2D Gaussian position errors (1mm-256mm) to guide the design of low-cost motion tracking solutions.

papers.title: Near-distance raw and reconstructed ground based SAR data
papers.summary: Addresses material classification from sparse radar data by releasing a dataset (RealSAR) containing raw GBSAR echoes and their corresponding Omega-K reconstructed images.

papers.title: Sim2Radar: Toward Bridging the Radar Sim-to-Real Gap with VLM-Guided Scene Reconstruction
papers.summary: Addresses the scarcity and high annotation costs of real-world radar datasets by proposing Sim2Radar, an end-to-end framework that synthesizes mmWave radar data directly from single-view RGB images.  It utilizes Vision-Language Models (VLMs) alongside depth estimation to reconstruct material-aware 3D scenes, then applies a physics-based ray tracer to simulate electromagnetic propagation. Pre-training on this synthetic data improves downstream real-world 3D radar object detection by up to +3.7 3D AP, effectively bridging the sim-to-real gap.

papers.title: 3D Scene Rendering with Multimodal Gaussian Splatting
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of vision-only 3D Gaussian Splatting (GS) in adverse conditions by proposing a multimodal framework integrating RF sensing (automotive radar).  This approach utilizes sparse RF depth measurements to efficiently predict depth and generate a high-quality 3D point cloud for initializing Gaussian primitives, resulting in highly robust and efficient high-fidelity 3D scene rendering even under poor lighting or weather.

papers.title: SynthRM: A Synthetic Data Platform for Vision-Aided Mobile System Simulation
papers.summary: Addresses the geometric ambiguity and ill-posed nature of vision-aided wireless sensing by introducing SynthRM, a scalable synthetic data platform. It implements a Visible-Aligned-Surface simulation strategy to ensure pixel-level consistency between visual semantics and electromagnetic response, transforming cross-view signal inference into a physically well-posed problem.

papers.title: UTDT: A Baseline Benchmark for Urban Traffic Flow Digital Twins via Roadside LiDAR–Camera Fusion
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Addresses the limitation of single-scene simulations in intelligent transportation by proposing UTDT, a high-fidelity Digital Twin framework. It integrates roadside LiDAR and camera data across multiple intersections to ensure spatiotemporal consistency and continuous vehicle identities, enabling accurate trajectory reconstruction for autonomous driving testing.

papers.title: Teaching AI to be a Picky Eater: Why Synthetic Data Quality Matters in Wireless Sensing
papers.summary: Explains the fundamental principle of wireless sensing, where human movements and breathing induce measurable disturbances in radio signals (e.g., Wi-Fi, mmWave). This allows devices to function as privacy-preserving sensors for smart homes and healthcare without relying on cameras.

papers.title: KuRALS: Ku-Band Radar Datasets for Multi-Scene Long-Range Surveillance with Baselines and Loss Design
papers.summary: English: Addresses the lack of diverse, long-range radar surveillance datasets by introducing KuRALS, a Ku-band Range-Doppler dataset covering aerial (UAVs), land (pedestrians/cars), and maritime (boats) scenarios, and proposes a lightweight semantic segmentation baseline with a novel interference-suppression loss that significantly outperforms existing methods.

papers.title: Wideband RF Radiance Field Modeling Using Frequency-embedded 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.summary: Models wideband RF propagation in 3D scenes by embedding frequency information into Gaussian splatting representations for accurate multi-frequency signal prediction.

papers.title: Talk is Not Always Cheap: Promoting Wireless Sensing Models with Text Prompts
papers.summary: Enhances wireless sensing model performance by leveraging text prompts and large language models to provide semantic guidance for radar data interpretation.

papers.title: One Snapshot is All You Need: A Generalized Method for mmWave Signal Generation
papers.summary: Generates diverse mmWave signals from a single snapshot by learning generalizable representations that capture signal patterns across different scenarios.

papers.title: Synthetic Radar Signal Generator for Human Motion Analysis
papers.summary: Synthesizes realistic radar signals for human motion scenarios by integrating motion-capture data with radar signal models to overcome data scarcity.

papers.title: Diffusion^2: Turning 3D Environments into Radio Frequency Heatmaps
papers.summary: Predicts RF signal propagation patterns in 3D environments using cascaded diffusion models that generate spatially-aware channel heatmaps.

papers.title: Inverse Rendering of Near-Field mmWave MIMO Radar for Material Reconstruction
papers.summary: Reconstructs material properties of objects from near-field radar measurements by formulating an inverse rendering problem that recovers dielectric characteristics.

papers.title: Simulate Any Radar: Attribute-Controllable Radar Simulation via Waveform Parameter Embedding
papers.summary: Enables controllable radar simulation across different configurations by embedding waveform parameters into generative models for flexible data augmentation.

papers.title: L2RDaS: Synthesizing 4D Radar Tensors for Model Generalization via Dataset Expansion
papers.summary: Improves model generalization by synthesizing realistic 4D radar tensors through learning-to-learn approaches that expand training dataset diversity.

papers.title: MITO: A Millimeter-Wave Dataset and Simulator for Non-Line-of-Sight Perception
papers.summary: Provides a comprehensive dataset and physics-based simulator for NLOS radar perception enabling research on seeing around corners with synthetic aperture techniques.

papers.title: RadaRays: Real-time Simulation of Rotating FMCW Radar for Mobile Robotics via Hardware-accelerated Ray Tracing
papers.summary: Develops a real-time radar simulator that uses hardware-accelerated ray tracing to more accurately model complex wave phenomena like reflection and scattering, overcoming the limitations of simplistic, lidar-like simulations in existing platforms.

papers.title: RF-Diffusion: Radio Signal Generation via Time-Frequency Diffusion
papers.summary: Generates realistic radio signals by applying diffusion models in the time-frequency domain to capture complex signal characteristics and variations.

papers.title: Generative Artificial Intelligence Meets Synthetic Aperture Radar: A survey
papers.publication: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
papers.summary: Surveys the intersection of generative AI techniques with SAR imaging, highlighting advancements in data synthesis, super-resolution, and anomaly detection.

papers.title: RadSimReal: Bridging the Gap Between Synthetic and Real Data in Radar Object Detection With Simulation
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Solves the radar data annotation challenge by proposing a fast physical simulator that bypasses the need for proprietary radar design details and produces synthetic data capable of matching or exceeding real-data training performance.

papers.title: Generation of Realistic Synthetic Raw Radar Data for Automated Driving Applications using Generative Adversarial Networks
papers.summary: Addresses the speed and noise-modeling limitations of ray tracing by using a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to generate realistic, raw radar sensor data for data augmentation in critical scenarios.

papers.title: An Open Framework to Model Diffraction by Dynamic Blockers in Millimeter Wave Simulations
papers.publication: Mediterranean Communication and Computer Networking Conference (MedComNet)
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of simulating multiple, dynamic blockers in mmWave channels by releasing an open-source framework that models diffraction and interfaces with existing ray-tracing software.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Sensing and Fusion in mmWave Beamforming for Connected Vehicles: A Transformer Based Framework
papers.summary: Addresses high beam training overheads and latency in connected vehicles by proposing a Transformer-based multi-modal sensing and fusion framework.  By utilizing modality-specific encoders and cross-modal attention to proactively predict optimal line-of-sight beams, it achieves 96.72% accuracy (top-15 beams) and reduces latency by 86.81% in real-world V2I/V2V scenarios compared to standard methods.

papers.title: Unsupervised Federated Learning With Harmonized Foundation Models for FMCW Radar-Based Hand Gesture Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of hardware heterogeneity in radar gesture recognition by proposing a radar-specific adaptation framework. This approach features a Harmonization Module to align signal dimensions across devices and utilizes an unsupervised federated learning pipeline to refine a transformer-based foundation model using only unlabeled data, achieving over 94% accuracy with minimal labeled samples.

papers.title: RF-MAE: A Self-Supervised Adaptive Frequency Masked Autoencoder With Radio-Frequency Signal Processing Applications
papers.summary: Addresses the heavy reliance on labeled datasets and limited generalization in RF signal processing by proposing RF-MAE, a self-supervised adaptive frequency masked autoencoder. It utilizes a novel Adaptive Frequency Masked (AFM) strategy to dynamically mask frequency components based on energy distribution, allowing the model to learn intrinsic patterns from unlabeled data and significantly improve performance across diverse RF tasks.

papers.title: Unsupervised Radar Point Cloud Enhancement via Arbitrary LiDAR Guided Diffusion Prior
papers.summary: Addresses the resolution limits of radar and the data constraints of paired training by introducing an unsupervised radar point cloud enhancement algorithm. This method formulates angle estimation as an inverse problem and employs an arbitrary LiDAR-guided diffusion model as a prior. This approach eliminates the need for paired data while achieving high fidelity and superior generalization compared to traditional paired methods.

papers.title: IQUMamba-1D: a Mamba-enhanced 1D U-Net for single-channel communication signal blind source separation
papers.publication: Journal of King Saud University Computer and Information Sciences
papers.summary: Overcomes the limitations of deep learning in single-channel blind source separation for long communication signals by proposing IQUMamba-1D, a Mamba-enhanced 1D U-Net that features adaptive dual tokenization, complex-valued state space modeling, and adaptive skip connections to ensure phase coherence and linear complexity.

papers.title: IoT-LLM: A framework for enhancing large language model reasoning from real-world sensor data
papers.summary: Enhances Large Language Models' capacity for physical-world reasoning by introducing IoT-LLM, a framework that integrates preprocessed sensor data, IoT-specific retrieval-augmented generation, and chain-of-thought prompting to significantly improve performance on real-world sensory tasks.

papers.title: Unlocking Interpretability for RF Sensing: A Complex-Valued White-Box Transformer
papers.summary: Develops an interpretable transformer architecture for RF sensing that processes complex-valued signals while maintaining transparency in feature learning and decision-making.

papers.title: Multi-View Radar Detection Transformer with Differentiable Positional Encoding
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
papers.summary: Enhances multi-view radar detection by introducing differentiable positional encodings that adapt to radar geometry and improve cross-view feature aggregation.

papers.title: Towards Foundational Models for Single-Chip Radar
papers.summary: Builds foundation models for single-chip radar through self-supervised pre-training on diverse radar data to enable transfer learning across multiple sensing tasks.

papers.title: SpikingRTNH: Spiking Neural Network for 4D Radar Object Detection
papers.summary: Achieves energy-efficient 4D radar object detection using spiking neural networks that exploit temporal sparsity in radar signals for low-power processing.

papers.title: Talk2Radar: Bridging Natural Language with 4D mmWave Radar for 3D Referring Expression Comprehension
papers.summary: Establishes a new research direction by creating the first-ever dataset and a baseline model that connects natural language expressions to objects within 4D mmWave radar point clouds for 3D referring expression comprehension.

papers.title: FMCW Radar Sensing for Indoor Drones Using Learned Representations
papers.summary: Addresses the inefficiency of task-specific deep learning for radar by using unsupervised learning to generate reusable, low-dimensional representations from raw FMCW data for various downstream tasks.

papers.title: ReSPEC: A Framework for Online Multispectral Sensor Reconfiguration in Dynamic Environments
papers.summary: Addresses the resource waste and rigidity of static multi-sensor fusion in robotics by proposing ReSPEC, a framework for online multispectral sensor reconfiguration.  It unifies sensing, learning, and actuation into a closed loop where a detection backbone scores the contribution of each modality (RGB, IR, mmWave, depth). A Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent then uses these scores to dynamically adjust sensor configurations—such as sampling frequency and resolution—in real time, successfully reducing GPU load by 29.3% with only a 5.3% accuracy drop on a mobile rover.

papers.title: Event Camera Meets Mobile Embodied Perception: Abstraction,Algorithm, Acceleration, Application
papers.summary: Provides a comprehensive survey (2014-2025) of event-based vision for agile mobile robots, detailing key abstractions, algorithms, hardware accelerations, and applications while highlighting the challenges of noise and real-time processing on resource-constrained platforms.

papers.title: OmniVLA: Physically-Grounded Multimodal VLA with Unified Multi-Sensor Perception for Robotic Manipulation
papers.summary: Solves the challenge of fusing diverse non-visual sensors (IR, radar, audio) into RGB-pretrained VLA models by creating a "sensor-masked image," a unified representation that overlays physical sensor data onto the RGB image.

papers.title: Towards Robust and Secure Embodied AI: A Survey on Vulnerabilities and Attacks
papers.summary: Comprehensively surveys security vulnerabilities in embodied AI systems including sensor spoofing and adversarial attacks on perception and decision-making modules.

papers.title: FuseGrasp: Radar-Camera Fusion for Robotic Grasping of Transparent Objects
papers.summary: Enables robust grasping of transparent objects by fusing radar material sensing with camera visual information to overcome camera limitations on reflective surfaces.

papers.title: Non-Line-of-Sight 3D Object Reconstruction via mmWave Surface Normal Estimation
papers.summary: Reconstructs hidden 3D objects by estimating surface normals from mmWave multipath reflections using synthetic aperture radar imaging principles.

papers.title: Loosely coupled 4D-Radar-Inertial Odometry for Ground Robots
papers.summary: Achieves robust ground robot odometry in challenging conditions through loosely-coupled fusion of 4D radar measurements with inertial sensor data.

papers.title: HyperDet: 3D Object Detection with Hyper 4D Radar Point Clouds
papers.summary: Addresses the performance gap between radar-only and LiDAR-based 3D object detection by proposing HyperDet, a detector-agnostic framework.  It constructs dense, noise-filtered "hyper 4D radar point clouds" by aggregating multi-radar surround-view data, applying cross-sensor consensus validation to suppress multipath noise, and utilizing a foreground-focused diffusion module to densify structures. Evaluated on the MAN TruckScenes dataset, it significantly improves the performance of standard LiDAR-oriented detectors (e.g., VoxelNeXt, CenterPoint) using only radar inputs.

papers.title: SDCM: Simulated Densifying and Compensatory Modeling Fusion for Radar-Vision 3-D Object Detection in Internet of Vehicles
papers.summary: Addresses the sparsity of 4-D radar point clouds and the representation degradation of vision data in challenging IoV environments by proposing the SDCM framework.  It features a Simulated Densifying (SimDen) module that uses 3-D Kernel Density Estimation and Gaussian simulation to densify radar points, a Radar Compensatory Mapping (RCM) module to mitigate visual degradation, and a Mamba Modeling Interactive Fusion (MMIF) module to reduce cross-modal heterogeneity. This approach achieves state-of-the-art performance with fewer parameters and faster inference on the VoD, TJ4DRadSet, and Astyx HiRes 2019 datasets.

papers.title: DR-Net: Dual-Representation Network with Motion-Aware Augmentation for 3D Object Detection based on 4D Radars
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of current 4D radar object detection (information loss, underutilized velocity) by proposing DR-Net, a Dual-Representation Network with Motion-Aware Augmentation. It features a Dual-Representation Encoder (DRE) and Sampling Fusion Backbone (SFB) to combine pillar and point features, a Motion-Aware Augmentation (MAA) module for robust velocity utilization, and an auxiliary velocity supervision branch, achieving state-of-the-art performance on VoD and TJ4DRadSet datasets.

papers.title: RaCFusion: Improving Camera-Based 3D Object Detection via Radar-Assisted Hierarchical Refinement
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of symmetrical radar-camera fusion architectures by proposing RaCFusion, a framework that treats the camera as the primary detector and enhances it via radar-assisted hierarchical refinement. It employs a Radar-assisted Query Generation module to create hybrid queries with spatial info and a Radar-assisted Velocity Aggregation module to refine features using radar velocity clues, achieving competitive performance on the nuScenes benchmark.

papers.title: MF-Radar: A Millimeter-Wave Radar Object Detection Network Based on Frequency-Enhanced Mamba
papers.summary: Addresses the high noise, sparse semantics, and computational overhead of mmWave radar object detection by proposing MF-Radar, a U-shaped Mamba network featuring a Multi-branch Attention Separable (MAS) module and an FFT-Enhanced Mamba block. It achieves superior performance on CRUW and CARRADA datasets while reducing parameters by 75% and GFLOPs by 72%.

papers.title: MiHazeFree3D: 3D Bounding Box Prediction for Vehicles and Pedestrians in Fog and Low-Light Conditions
papers.publication: ACM Transactions on Internet of Things
papers.summary: Addresses the failure of camera and LiDAR systems in adverse weather by presenting MiHazeFree3D, a mmWave radar-based system that uses a deep learning architecture with feature fusion to overcome signal reflection and motion errors, enabling reliable 3D bounding box prediction for vehicles and pedestrians in fog and low-light conditions.

papers.title: Temporal Micro-Doppler Spectrogram-based ViT Multiclass Target Classification
papers.summary: Addresses the classification of overlapping and partially occluded targets by employing a Temporal Vision Transformer that processes stacked range-velocity-angle tensors with mobility-aware constraints to explicitly model sequential kinematic features.

papers.title: SSMRadNet : A Sample-wise State-Space Framework for Efficient and Ultra-Light Radar Segmentation and Object Detection
papers.summary: Proposes the first State Space Model (SSM) for raw radar data that sequentially processes samples at both the chirp and frame level, drastically reducing computational cost and model size compared to CNN/Transformer methods.

papers.title: Pillar-Based Adaptive Sparse Transformer with Cost-Optimized Positive Sample Selection for 4D Radar Object Detection
papers.publication: International Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems Research
papers.summary: Solves the limited receptive field of 3D convnets by using a sparse pillar transformer (for long-range context) and addresses poor label assignment by using a novel cross-label head to ensure balanced positive samples.

papers.title: M^3Detection: Multi-Frame Multi-Level Feature Fusion for Multi-Modal 3D Object Detection with Camera and 4D Imaging Radar
papers.summary: Solves the problem of incomplete single-frame data by proposing a multi-frame fusion framework that efficiently aggregates features at both the global-object level (guided by radar) and local-grid level (guided by trajectories).

papers.title: SFGFusion: Surface Fitting Guided 3D Object Detection with 4D Radar and Camera Fusion
papers.summary: Overcomes radar sparsity by using a quadratic surface fitting model to generate dense depth, which in turn guides the image-to-BEV transformation and creates dense pseudo-points.

papers.title: DA3D: Domain-Aware Dynamic Adaptation for All-Weather Multimodal 3D Detection
papers.summary: Addresses feature-level domain shifts in multi-sensor fusion by using LoRA as a dynamic "capacity controller" to efficiently reallocate model parameters based on weather difficulty.

papers.title: Doppler-Aware LiDAR-RADAR Fusion for Weather-Robust 3D Detection
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Solves the problem of poor Doppler encoding in existing fusion methods by introducing a multi-path iterative module that uses Doppler to highlight dynamic regions, which in turn progressively refines both RADAR power and LiDAR features.

papers.title: RICCARDO: Radar Hit Prediction and Convolution for Camera-Radar 3D Object Detection
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Enhances camera-radar fusion by predicting where radar hits should occur on objects and performing convolutions in predicted hit space for improved 3D detection.

papers.title: DoppDrive: Doppler-Driven Temporal Aggregation for Improved Radar Object Detection
papers.summary: Improves radar object detection by using Doppler velocity measurements to guide temporal aggregation of radar frames for motion-aware feature learning.

papers.title: RadarNeXt: Real-Time and Reliable 3D Object Detector Based On 4D mmWave Imaging Radar
papers.summary: Achieves real-time 3D object detection from 4D radar by designing efficient architectures that balance accuracy and computational efficiency for automotive deployment.

papers.title: RADLER: Radar Object Detection Leveraging Semantic 3D City Models and Self-Supervised Learning
papers.summary: Improves radar object detection by leveraging semantic 3D city models as prior knowledge and using self-supervised learning to reduce annotation requirements.

papers.title: Beyond Pillars: Advancing 3D Object Detection with Salient Voxel Enhancement of LiDAR-4D Radar Fusion
papers.summary: Advances beyond pillar-based representations by identifying and enhancing salient voxels in LiDAR-radar fusion for more discriminative 3D object features.

papers.title: RCDFNet: A 4-D Radar and Camera Dual-Level Fusion Network for 3-D Object Detection
papers.summary: Performs radar-camera fusion at both feature and decision levels to complement strengths of each modality for robust 3D object detection across conditions.

papers.title: V2X-R: Cooperative LiDAR-4D Radar Fusion for 3D Object Detection with Denoising Diffusion
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Enables cooperative perception in V2X scenarios by fusing LiDAR and 4D radar across vehicles using denoising diffusion to handle communication noise.

papers.title: RCDINO: Enhancing Radar-Camera 3D Object Detection with DINOv2 Semantic Features
papers.summary: Enhances radar-camera 3D detection by incorporating DINOv2's rich semantic features to provide better object understanding and context awareness.

papers.title: CenterRadarNet: Joint 3D Object Detection and Tracking Framework Using 4D FMCW Radar
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Image Processing (ICIP)
papers.summary: Overcomes the limitations of 3D radar tensors by using 4D (elevation) radar data in a joint architecture that simultaneously learns representations for both 3D object detection and re-identification.

papers.title: E-RODNet: Lightweight Approach to Object Detection by Vehicular Millimeter-Wave Radar
papers.summary: Addresses the excessive complexity of current radar perception models by proposing a lightweight ConvFormer-based architecture that integrates a global feature fusion module to achieve SOTA performance with minimal computational cost.

papers.title: Bootstrapping Autonomous Driving Radars with Self-Supervised Learning
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Solves the radar annotation bottleneck by using a self-supervised framework with combined radar-to-radar and radar-to-vision contrastive losses to pre-train models on unlabeled data.

papers.title: LEROjD: Lidar Extended Radar-Only Object Detection
papers.summary: Improves radar-only object detection by proposing two training strategies (multi-stage LiDAR thin-out and knowledge distillation) that transfer knowledge from LiDAR data without requiring architecture changes.

papers.title: DPFT: Dual Perspective Fusion Transformer for Camera-Radar-based Object Detection
papers.summary: Solves the radar sparsity problem by using the raw radar cube instead of point clouds, and effectively leverages elevation information by fusing features in both camera and ground-plane perspectives.

papers.title: RaLiBEV: Radar and LiDAR BEV Fusion Learning for Anchor Box Free Object Detection Systems
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
papers.summary: Addresses the weak feature interaction and inconsistent regression of existing LiDAR-radar fusion methods by introducing an interactive transformer module and an advanced label assignment strategy.

papers.title: Robust 3D Object Detection from LiDAR-Radar Point Clouds Via Cross-Modal Feature Augmentation
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Improves the performance of single-modal 3D detectors (LiDAR-only or radar-only) by training them with a cross-modal "hallucination" framework that uses spatial and feature alignment to refine each sensor's backbone with knowledge from the other.

papers.title: Enhanced K-Radar: Optimal Density Reduction to Improve Detection Performance and Accessibility of 4D Radar Tensor-based Object Detection
papers.summary: Addresses the high memory cost of 4D radar tensors by empirically determining an optimal density reduction level (5%) and introducing an offline sparse representation (4DSRT) to accelerate training.

papers.title: Utilizing Super-Resolution for Enhanced Automotive Radar Object Detection
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Image Processing (ICIP)
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of paired high/low-resolution radar data by first training a super-resolution model on simulated data, and then using this model to enhance a real-world dataset for object detection.

papers.title: Radar Range–Doppler Flow: A Radar Signal Processing Technique to Enhance Radar Target Classification
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
papers.summary: Solves the failure of traditional radar clustering (when targets are close and at similar speeds) by introducing radial acceleration, extracted via "range–Doppler flow," as an additional distinguishing feature.

papers.title: RC-ROSNet: Fusing 3D Radar Range-Angle Heat Maps and Camera Images for Radar Object Segmentation
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
papers.summary: Performs radar object segmentation by fusing 3D range-angle heatmaps with camera images to leverage complementary spatial and semantic information.

papers.title: M2CNet: LiDAR 3D Semantic Segmentation Based on Multi-level Multi-view Cross-attention Fusion for Autonomous Vehicles
papers.summary: Enhances LiDAR semantic segmentation through multi-level multi-view cross-attention that captures both local details and global context for autonomous driving.

papers.title: RadarMask: A Novel End-to-End Sparse Millimeter-Wave Radar Sequence Panoptic Segmentation and Tracking Method
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Performs end-to-end panoptic segmentation and tracking on sparse mmWave radar sequences by jointly learning instance masks and temporal associations.

papers.title: RETR: Multi-View Radar Detection Transformer for Indoor Perception
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Achieves robust indoor perception by aggregating multi-view radar data through transformer architecture that handles varying spatial perspectives and occlusions.

papers.title: 4D Radar And Vision Fusion Detection Model Based On Segmentation-assisted
papers.summary: Improves 4D radar-vision fusion detection by using segmentation results as intermediate supervision to guide feature learning and alignment between modalities.

papers.title: AdaPKC: PeakConv with Adaptive Peak Receptive Field for Radar Semantic Segmentation
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Addresses radar sparsity in semantic segmentation through adaptive peak convolutions that dynamically adjust receptive fields based on local point density.

papers.title: TARSS-Net: Temporal-Aware Radar Semantic Segmentation Network
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Enhances radar semantic segmentation by explicitly modeling temporal dependencies across consecutive frames to leverage motion information for better predictions.

papers.title: Achelous++: Power-Oriented Water-Surface Panoptic Perception Framework on Edge Devices based on Vision-Radar Fusion and Pruning of Heterogeneous Modalities
papers.summary: Addresses the high power consumption of water-surface perception models by proposing a lightweight, vision-radar fusion framework with a novel multi-modal pruning strategy to enable efficient multi-task learning on edge devices.

papers.title: ASY-VRNet: Waterway Panoptic Driving Perception Model based on Asymmetric Fair Fusion of Vision and 4D mmWave Radar
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Addresses the differing feature requirements of object detection and semantic segmentation by proposing an asymmetric fusion module that treats both vision and radar as point sets to create a task-specific shared feature space.

papers.title: RadarCam-Depth: Radar-Camera Fusion for Depth Estimation with Learned Metric Scale
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Addresses the artifacts of direct camera-radar data fusion by proposing a four-stage pipeline that uses sparse radar to learn a dense, refined metric scale map, which is then applied to correct a monocular depth prediction.

papers.title: The ADUULM-360 Dataset - A Multi-Modal Dataset for Depth Estimation in Adverse Weather
papers.publication: IEEE 27th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of adverse weather scenes in depth estimation datasets by releasing a comprehensive 360-degree, multi-modal (camera, LiDAR, radar) dataset to benchmark and expose the limitations of current methods in such conditions.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Supervision Based Road Segmentation and Trajectory Prediction With Automotive Radar
papers.summary: Solves the radar annotation bottleneck by using DGPS as a cross-modal supervisor to automatically generate labels for training a multi-task network for road segmentation and trajectory prediction.

papers.title: RIDERS: Radar-Infrared Depth Estimation for Robust Sensing
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Solves the challenge of depth estimation in adverse weather by fusing a monocular infrared camera with radar, using the sparse radar data to learn a dense scale map that corrects the infrared-based depth prediction.

papers.title: TransRadar: Adaptive-Directional Transformer for Real-Time Multi-View Radar Semantic Segmentation
papers.publication: Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
papers.summary: Addresses the challenges of radar sparsity and noise in semantic segmentation by introducing an adaptive-directional transformer that captures long-range dependencies while focusing on relevant spatial regions.

papers.title: PeakConv: Learning Peak Receptive Field for Radar Semantic Segmentation
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Addresses the mismatch of standard convolutions with radar signals by introducing a "Peak Convolution" (PeakConv) inspired by the classic CFAR principle of detecting local peak responses.

papers.title: Multi-View Radar Semantic Segmentation
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Proposes lightweight architectures that analyze multiple 2D "views" (projections) of the raw range-angle-Doppler tensor to achieve efficient and accurate radar semantic segmentation.

papers.title: RaLiFlow: Scene Flow Estimation with 4D Radar and LiDAR Point Clouds
papers.summary: Fills the research gap in fusing 4D radar and LiDAR for scene flow by constructing a dedicated dataset and proposing RaLiFlow, a framework that employs a Dynamic-aware Bidirectional Cross-modal Fusion (DBCF) module to effectively leverage radar's dynamic cues while mitigating noise via specialized loss functions.

papers.title: RadarMP: Motion Perception for 4D mmWave Radar in Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Addresses the imprecision of decoupled motion perception in sparse radar data by using a unified architecture that jointly models detection and scene flow from low-level echoes, leveraging Doppler-guided self-supervision to eliminate the need for annotations.

papers.title: Self-Supervised Diffusion-Based Scene Flow Estimation and Motion Segmentation with 4D Radar
papers.summary: Estimates scene flow and segments moving objects from 4D radar using self-supervised diffusion models that learn motion patterns without manual annotations.

papers.title: DoGFlow: Self-Supervised LiDAR Scene Flow via Cross-Modal Doppler Guidance
papers.summary: Overcomes the bottleneck of manual annotation for LiDAR scene flow by using a self-supervised method that generates pseudo-labels from radar Doppler measurements, significantly improving performance and data efficiency.

papers.title: milliFlow: Scene Flow Estimation on mmWave Radar Point Cloud for Human Motion Sensing
papers.summary: Uses deep learning to estimate scene flow from mmWave point clouds, creating a new intermediate motion representation that boosts performance on downstream tasks like human activity recognition and parsing.

papers.title: HeLiMOS: A Dataset for Moving Object Segmentation in 3D Point Clouds From Heterogeneous LiDAR Sensors
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of MOS datasets for diverse (e.g., solid-state) LiDARs by releasing a new heterogeneous dataset (HeLiMOS) generated using a novel automatic labeling pipeline.

papers.title: Multi-Session Mapping and Long-Term Localization for Autonomous Vehicles Using Radar
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of LiDAR (weather sensitivity, cost) in long-term autonomous navigation by proposing a radar-based multi-session mapping and localization framework. This approach utilizes a multi-sequence loop detection strategy for robust trajectory alignment, a novel map maintenance module to filter noise and retain persistent features, and scan-to-map matching to ensure reliable pose estimation across different sessions.

papers.title: Super4DR: 4D Radar-centric Self-supervised Odometry and Gaussian-based Map Optimization
papers.summary: Overcomes the sparsity and noise of 4D radar SLAM by designing a cluster-aware self-supervised odometry network and a 3D Gaussian-based mapping strategy that recovers blurry/undetected structures, achieving near-supervised accuracy and high-quality multi-modal rendering.

papers.title: S3E: Self-Supervised State Estimation for Radar-Inertial System
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Bypasses sparse point clouds by using richer radar spectra and fuses inertial data in a self-supervised manner, employing a novel cross-fusion technique to overcome poor angular resolution.

papers.title: AF-RLIO: Adaptive Fusion of Radar-LiDAR-Inertial Information for Robust Odometry in Challenging Environments
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Improves multi-sensor odometry by using radar to not only provide complementary data but also to actively de-noise LiDAR and determine its reliability, enabling an adaptive fusion for robust navigation in degraded conditions.

papers.title: RINO: Accurate, Robust Radar-Inertial Odometry With Non-Iterative Estimation
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
papers.summary: Achieves accurate radar-inertial odometry by introducing a non-iterative estimation framework that reduces computational complexity while maintaining robustness against radar noise and outliers.

papers.title: Digital Beamforming Enhanced Radar Odometry
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Improves radar odometry accuracy by applying digital beamforming to enhance angular resolution and reduce multipath interference for better feature matching.

papers.title: DRO: Doppler-Aware Direct Radar Odometry
papers.summary: Achieves accurate direct radar odometry by explicitly incorporating Doppler velocity measurements into optimization framework to constrain ego-motion estimation.

papers.title: GaRLIO: Gravity enhanced Radar-LiDAR-Inertial Odometry
papers.summary: Enhances multi-sensor odometry by incorporating gravity direction as additional constraint to improve radar-LiDAR-inertial fusion accuracy and robustness.

papers.title: Ground-Optimized 4D Radar-Inertial Odometry via Continuous Velocity Integration using Gaussian Process
papers.summary: Optimizes radar-inertial odometry for ground vehicles by using Gaussian processes to model continuous velocity integration and handle ground constraint.

papers.title: Equi-RO: A 4D mmWave Radar Odometry via Equivariant Networks
papers.summary: Achieves robust radar odometry by leveraging equivariant neural networks that respect geometric transformations and improve generalization across diverse motion patterns.

papers.title: Learning Point Correspondences In Radar 3D Point Clouds For Radar-Inertial Odometry
papers.summary: Improves radar-inertial odometry by using a self-supervised Transformer network to find robust point correspondences, uniquely generating training data by solving a linear sum assignment problem to avoid manual labeling.

papers.title: Radar4VoxMap: Accurate Odometry from Blurred Radar Observations
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Tackles the issue of submap blurring from cumulative errors in radar odometry by using an RCS-weighted voxel map and a fixed-lag graph optimization that jointly refines both the map and the vehicle's pose.

papers.title: EFEAR-4D：Ego-velocity Filtering for Efficient and Accurate 4D radar Odometry
papers.summary: Improves 4D radar odometry efficiency by filtering radar points based on ego-velocity consistency to remove dynamic objects and outliers.

papers.title: RadarMOSEVE: A Spatial-Temporal Transformer Network for Radar-Only Moving Object Segmentation and Ego-Velocity Estimation
papers.summary: Proposes a Transformer network with custom self- and cross-attention mechanisms designed to leverage radar's radial velocity information to overcome data sparsity and noise for simultaneous moving object segmentation and ego-velocity estimation.

papers.title: DeRO: Dead Reckoning Based on Radar Odometry With Accelerometers Aided for Robot Localization
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Mitigates localization drift by using radar Doppler velocity and gyroscope data for direct dead reckoning, while using accelerometer-derived tilt angles and scan matching for Kalman filter updates, thus avoiding error-prone accelerometer double integration.

papers.title: Continuous-Time Radar-Inertial and Lidar-Inertial Odometry Using a Gaussian Process Motion Prior
papers.summary: Enables continuous-time radar-inertial odometry for a spinning radar by using a Gaussian process motion prior and IMU preintegration within a sliding-window batch estimator.

papers.title: A Robust Baro-Radar-Inertial Odometry M-Estimator for Multicopter Navigation in Cities and Forests
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Multisensor Fusion and Integration for Intelligent Systems (MFI)
papers.summary: Enhances multicopter navigation in challenging environments by fusing barometer, radar, and inertial data using an M-estimator framework that robustly handles outliers and sensor noise.

papers.title: Successive Pose Estimation and Beam Tracking for mmWave Vehicular Communication Systems
papers.summary: Alleviates beam training overhead in vehicular mmWave communications by first estimating the vehicle's pose via radar odometry and then using this information within an Extended Kalman Filter to proactively track the communication beam.

papers.title: Open-RadVLAD: Fast and Robust Radar Place Recognition
papers.summary: Achieves robust and efficient radar place recognition by applying a 1D Fourier Transform along radial power returns for partial translational invariance and a VLAD descriptor for rotational invariance.

papers.title: Degradation Resilient LiDAR-Radar-Inertial Odometry
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Mitigates LiDAR odometry failure by using a tightly-coupled factor graph that allows LiDAR to contribute partial information along its reliable axes, while radar and inertial data provide complementary support.

papers.title: OORD: The Oxford Offroad Radar Dataset
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Fills the gap of urban-centric data by releasing a large-scale radar dataset (OORD) collected in rugged, extreme-weather offroad environments to benchmark place recognition.

papers.title: Co-RaL: Complementary Radar-Leg Odometry with 4-DoF Optimization and Rolling Contact
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Improves legged robot localization by tightly coupling radar and leg odometry, using 4-DoF optimization to enhance the radar's z-axis accuracy and rolling contact modeling to make the leg kinematics robust.

papers.title: RIs-Calib: An Open-Source Spatiotemporal Calibrator for Multiple 3-D Radars and IMUs Based on Continuous-Time Estimation
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Solves infrastructure-free spatiotemporal calibration for multi-radar/multi-IMU systems by first initializing parameters using continuous-time B-splines and then refining them via global batch optimization.

papers.title: Advancements in Radar Odometry
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Improves a state-of-the-art radar odometry pipeline by introducing Gaussian smoothing and ICP alignment for better local scene understanding, and validates its enhanced accuracy through the first-ever comprehensive benchmark on the Boreas dataset.

papers.title: Enhancing Doppler Ego-Motion Estimation: A Temporally Weighted Approach to RANSAC
papers.summary: Addresses high outlier rates in Doppler ego-motion estimation by modifying RANSAC to use a temporally weighted sliding window, giving higher priority to more recent measurements.

papers.title: Radar4Motion: IMU-Free 4D Radar Odometry With Robust Dynamic Filtering and RCS-Weighted Matching
papers.summary: Achieves IMU-free radar odometry by leveraging Doppler data for initial ego-motion estimation and RCS values to weight points during scan-to-submap matching, addressing data sparsity.

papers.title: Multi-Target Device-Free Positioning Based on Spatial-Temporal mmWave Point Cloud
papers.summary: Solves the challenge of multi-target device-free positioning by using a spatial-temporal clustering method and a gait-based particle filter to associate and track identities from fine-grained mmWave point clouds.

papers.title: Do we need scan-matching in radar odometry?
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Demonstrates that for 4D radar, odometry derived from the direct integration of Doppler and IMU data can be similar or superior to traditional 3D scan registration, especially in feature-sparse environments.

papers.title: Traj-LO: In Defense of LiDAR-Only Odometry Using an Effective Continuous-Time Trajectory
papers.summary: Improves LiDAR-only odometry by using a continuous-time trajectory representation that effectively handles motion distortion and allows for accurate pose interpolation between scans.

papers.title: Doppler-only Single-scan 3D Vehicle Odometry
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Achieves vehicle odometry using only Doppler velocity measurements from a single radar scan by formulating a novel optimization problem that estimates 3D motion without relying on point cloud registration.

papers.title: MS-VRO: A Multistage Visual-Millimeter Wave Radar Fusion Odometry
papers.summary: Solves monocular VO's scale ambiguity, scale drift, and dynamic environment errors by deeply fusing mmWave radar data at multiple distinct stages: initialization, joint optimization, and feature selection.

papers.title: MSC-RAD4R: ROS-Based Automotive Dataset With 4D Radar
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of sufficient odometry sensors in existing 4D radar datasets by releasing a new, comprehensive dataset (MSC-RAD4R) specifically designed to support robust radar SLAM/odometry research.

papers.title: milliEgo: Single-chip mmWave Radar Aided Egomotion Estimation via Deep Sensor Fusion
papers.summary: Addresses the brittleness of traditional registration on sparse, single-chip radar data by proposing a deep architecture optimized for sparse pose transformation and a mixed-attention module for robust sensor fusion.

papers.title: Asynchronous Data Fusion With Randomly Delayed Measurements for Lane-Level Vehicle Tracking in Tunnel Environment
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Tackles unreliable lane-level vehicle tracking in tunnels by using a Bayesian weight mixture filter to fuse asynchronous, randomly delayed measurements from MMW radar and magnetic sensors.

papers.title: USVTrack: USV-Based 4D Radar-Camera Tracking Dataset for Autonomous Driving in Inland Waterways
papers.summary: Provides the first 4D radar-camera tracking dataset for inland waterway autonomous navigation featuring unique challenges of water surface reflections.

papers.title: Real-Time Multi-object Tracking and Identification Using Sparse Point-Cloud Data from Low-Cost mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Enables real-time multi-object tracking from sparse mmWave point clouds through efficient data association algorithms optimized for low-cost radar sensors.

papers.title: RadarGen: Automotive Radar Point Cloud Generation from Cameras
papers.summary: Facilitates multimodal generative simulation by introducing RadarGen, a diffusion-based model that synthesizes realistic automotive radar point clouds directly from camera images, utilizing BEV representations and aligning with depth, semantic, and motion cues to ensure physical plausibility.

papers.title: RF-Based 3D SLAM Rivaling Vision Approaches
papers.publication: ACM International Conference on Mobile Systems, Applications, and Services (MobiSys)
papers.summary: Achieves centimeter-level 3D SLAM superior to vision baselines by introducing a training-free uncertainty quantification method to mitigate the inference instability inherent in learning-based RF imaging.

papers.title: RISE: Single Static Radar-based Indoor Scene Understanding
papers.summary: Overcomes the low spatial resolution of single static radar by exploiting multipath reflections through a bi-angular enhancement technique and applying a hierarchical diffusion framework to achieve accurate indoor layout reconstruction and object detection.

papers.title: Doppler-SLAM: Doppler-Aided Radar-Inertial and LiDAR-Inertial SLAM
papers.summary: Enhances SLAM accuracy by integrating Doppler velocity measurements into radar-inertial and LiDAR-inertial frameworks to provide additional motion constraints.

papers.title: S^3E: Self-Supervised State Estimation for Radar-Inertial System
papers.summary: Achieves self-supervised radar-inertial state estimation by learning motion patterns from unlabeled data to reduce dependency on ground truth annotations.

papers.title: MapKD: Unlocking Prior Knowledge with Cross-Modal Distillation for Efficient Online HD Map Construction
papers.summary: Enables efficient online HD map construction by distilling knowledge from offline maps using cross-modal learning to reduce computational overhead.

papers.title: Towards Dense and Accurate Radar Perception via Efficient Cross-Modal Diffusion Model
papers.summary: Generates dense radar representations from sparse measurements using cross-modal diffusion models that leverage complementary sensor information for accurate perception.

papers.title: Radarize: Enhancing Radar SLAM with Generalizable Doppler-Based Odometry
papers.publication: ACM International Conference on Mobile Systems, Applications, and Services (MobiSys)
papers.summary: Introduces a self-contained, radar-only SLAM system that significantly outperforms existing radar-inertial methods by leveraging Doppler-based odometry and multipath suppression, eliminating the need for any auxiliary sensors.

papers.title: Enhancing mmWave Radar Point Cloud via Visual-inertial Supervision
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Replaces expensive LiDAR supervision with a low-cost visual-inertial system to densify radar point clouds and remove artifacts, making high-quality radar enhancement accessible for commercial vehicles.

papers.title: RaNDT SLAM: Radar SLAM Based on Intensity-Augmented Normal Distributions Transform
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Tackles the slowness of radar SLAM in noisy environments by augmenting the Normal Distributions Transform (NDT) with radar intensity information for faster and more accurate registration.

papers.title: A Deep Automotive Radar Detector Using the RaDelft Dataset
papers.summary: Uses synchronized lidar data as ground truth to train a neural network that generates shape-preserving, lidar-like point clouds from only radar data.

papers.title: mmPlace: Robust Place Recognition With Intermediate Frequency Signal of Low-Cost Single-Chip Millimeter Wave Radar
papers.summary: Achieves robust place recognition using intermediate frequency signals from low-cost single-chip mmWave radar by extracting stable features that are invariant to environmental changes.

papers.title: BatMobility: Towards Flying Without Seeing for Autonomous Drones
papers.summary: Enables drones to 'fly without seeing' by introducing a radar-only perception system that uses a novel 'radio flow' technique as a robust, non-optical alternative to optical flow for navigation.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Contrastive Learning of Representations for Navigation using Lightweight, Low-Cost Millimeter Wave Radar for Adverse Environmental Conditions
papers.summary: Enables robust, radar-only reinforcement learning navigation in adverse conditions by using cross-modal contrastive learning to align sparse radar representations with clean LiDAR data during the training phase.

papers.title: RainSense: An Autonomous Driving Environmental Perception Dataset with Rain Intensity Annotations
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of detailed natural rainfall datasets by introducing RainSense, a multi-sensor autonomous driving dataset with fine-grained rain intensity annotations.  It provides synchronized camera, LiDAR, and 4D radar data across 728 cases, demonstrating that while heavy rain severely degrades camera and LiDAR perception, radar maintains exceptional stability and resilience.

papers.title: River2: A Tightly Coupled Multiradar Multi-IMU Velocity Estimator Based on Continuous-Time Optimization
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Addresses the need for robust ego-velocity estimation in complex environments by introducing River2, a tightly coupled multi-radar and multi-IMU framework based on continuous-time optimization. By extending B-spline optimization to multisensor setups, it ensures continuity even during single-sensor failures and expands the field of view, while incorporating outlier removal and zero-velocity updates (ZUPT) to achieve high accuracy and resilience.

papers.title: A Universal Framework for Extrinsic Calibration of Camera, Radar, and LiDAR
papers.summary: Addresses the scalability limits of pair-wise, target-dependent calibration by introducing a universal framework based on an Iterative Best Match (IBM) algorithm. This approach utilizes tracked natural targets (e.g., pedestrians) to establish cross-modal correspondences, enabling simultaneous, marker-free calibration of Camera, Radar, and LiDAR systems.

papers.title: Forging Spatial Intelligence: A Roadmap of Multi-Modal Data Pre-Training for Autonomous Systems
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of creating unified spatial understanding in autonomous systems by presenting a comprehensive framework for multi-modal pre-training (camera, LiDAR, etc.), offering a taxonomy of learning strategies, and outlining a roadmap for developing general-purpose foundation models capable of robust "Spatial Intelligence."

papers.title: Indoor FireRescue Radar: 4D Indoor Millimeter Wave Dataset and Analysis for Hazardous Environment Perception
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Provides the first comprehensive 4D mmWave radar dataset for indoor fire rescue scenarios, featuring diverse environmental conditions and annotated objects to facilitate research in hazardous environment perception.

papers.title: RoboDriveVLM: A Novel Benchmark and Baseline towards Robust Vision-Language Models for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Introduces a comprehensive benchmark (RoboDriveVLM) for evaluating vision-language models in autonomous driving, featuring diverse scenarios and tasks to promote robust multi-modal understanding.

papers.title: HENet++: Hybrid Encoding and Multi-task Learning for 3D Perception and End-to-end Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Addresses the high computational cost and conflicting feature needs of multi-task 3D perception by using a hybrid encoding scheme (large encoder for recent frames, small encoder for older frames) to efficiently extract both dense and sparse features.

papers.title: Layer-Wise Modality Decomposition for Interpretable Multimodal Sensor Fusion
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of interpretability in multi-sensor fusion models by proposing a post-hoc, model-agnostic method that decomposes the network layer by layer to attribute the final prediction to each individual input modality.

papers.title: Availability-aware Sensor Fusion via Unified Canonical Space for 4D Radar, LiDAR, and Camera
papers.summary: Addresses the vulnerability of multi-sensor fusion systems to sensor failure by projecting all sensor data into a unified representation, enabling robust cross-modal attention that is aware of sensor availability.

papers.title: Radar and Event Camera Fusion for Agile Robot Ego‑Motion Estimation
papers.summary: Proposes an IMU‑free, feature‐association‑free fusion of event camera and millimetre‐wave radar Doppler to directly estimate 6‑DOF velocity of agile robots under high‑motion conditions, bypassing traditional frame matching.

papers.title: Radar‑Based NLoS Pedestrian Localization for Darting‑Out Scenarios Near Parked Vehicles with Camera‑Assisted Point Cloud Interpretation
papers.summary: Tackles unexpected pedestrian appearance in NLoS urban parking‑vehicle blind spots by fusing monocular camera segmentation and depth estimation of parked vehicles with 2D radar point‑cloud data to accurately localize the pedestrian early.

papers.title: VR-PCT: Enhanced VR Semantic Performance via Edge-Client Collaborative Multi-modal Point Cloud Transformers
papers.summary: Reduces data overhead in multi-modal VR by using an edge-client collaborative framework where the client transmits only the "semantic region" of the visual data, not the full video, to the edge for fusion with radar.

papers.title: Talk2PC: Enhancing 3D Visual Grounding through LiDAR and Radar Point Clouds Fusion for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Achieves prompt-guided 3D visual grounding by introducing a two-stage adaptive fusion framework that combines LiDAR and 4D radar, using cross-attention and graph fusion to locate the queried object.

papers.title: V2XScenes: A Multiple Challenging Traffic Conditions Dataset for Large-Range Vehicle-Infrastructure Collaborative Perception
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of benchmark data for V2X perception in occluded scenarios by releasing a dataset that uniquely provides consistent, global object tracking IDs across successive road sections.

papers.title: 4D mmWave Radar for Sensing Enhancement in Adverse Environments: Advances and Challenges
papers.summary: Fills a research gap by providing the first comprehensive review of 4D mmWave radar datasets and learning-based methods specifically for perception in adverse environments.

papers.title: RadarRGBD: A Multi-Sensor Fusion Dataset for Perception with RGB-D and mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Provides a comprehensive RGB-D and mmWave radar fusion dataset enabling research on complementary depth sensing and material property estimation.

papers.title: MIPD: A Multi-Sensory Interactive Perception Dataset for Embodied Intelligent Driving
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of embodied sensory information in autonomous driving datasets by introducing a new dataset (MIPD) that integrates sound, light, and vibration with traditional sensors to foster more interactive and comprehensive perception.

papers.title: Artemis: Contour-Guided 3-D Sensing and Localization With mmWave Radar for Infrastructure-Assisted AVs
papers.summary: Achieves precise infrastructure-based localization by using mmWave radar to extract object contours that guide 3D sensing and vehicle positioning.

papers.title: CoVeRaP: Cooperative Vehicular Perception through mmWave FMCW Radars
papers.summary: Enables cooperative perception among vehicles by sharing and fusing mmWave FMCW radar data to expand sensing coverage and reduce occlusions.

papers.title: Ultra-High-Frequency Harmony: mmWave Radar and Event Camera Orchestrate Accurate Drone Landing
papers.summary: Achieves precise drone landing by fusing mmWave radar range measurements with event camera's high-temporal-resolution motion detection capabilities.

papers.title: Rehearse-3d: A Multi-Modal Emulated Rain Dataset for 3d Point Cloud De-Raining
papers.summary: Provides multi-modal rainy weather dataset enabling research on point cloud de-raining algorithms for robust perception under adverse conditions.

papers.title: 4D-ROLLS: 4D Radar Occupancy Learning via LiDAR Supervision
papers.summary: Learns 4D radar occupancy representations using LiDAR as supervisory signal to overcome radar annotation challenges and enable dense scene understanding.

papers.title: MetaOcc: Spatio-Temporal Fusion of Surround-View 4D Radar and Camera for 3D Occupancy Prediction with Dual Training Strategies
papers.summary: Predicts 3D occupancy by fusing surround-view 4D radar and camera through spatio-temporal networks with dual training strategies for improved generalization.

papers.title: WaterScenes: A Multi-Task 4D Radar-Camera Fusion Dataset and Benchmarks for Autonomous Driving on Water Surfaces
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of autonomous driving datasets for water surfaces by releasing WaterScenes, a large-scale 4D radar-camera fusion dataset with multi-task benchmarks.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Fusion Sensing: A Comprehensive Review of Millimeter-Wave Radar and Its Integration With Other Modalities
papers.summary: Comprehensively surveys mmWave radar fusion with various modalities covering fusion architectures, challenges, and applications across different domains.

papers.title: A Deep Automotive Radar Detector using the RaDelft Dataset
papers.summary: Uses synchronized lidar data as ground truth to train a neural network that generates shape-preserving, lidar-like point clouds from only radar data.

papers.title: Radar Meets Vision: Robustifying Monocular Metric Depth Prediction for Mobile Robotics
papers.summary: Solves the poor generalization of monocular depth estimation in low-texture robotics environments by encoding sparse mmWave radar data into the vision model's input.

papers.title: CARB-Net: Camera-Assisted Radar-Based Network for Vulnerable Road User Detection
papers.summary: Addresses radar's poor angular resolution in VRU detection by fusing camera information into the early stages of a radar network, using a context learning approach to ensure graceful degradation.

papers.title: A Resource Efficient Fusion Network for Object Detection in Bird's-Eye View using Camera and Raw Radar Data
papers.publication: IEEE 27th International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Solves the problem of radar sparsity by bypassing point clouds, instead fusing decoded Range-Azimuth (RA) features (from the raw RD spectrum) with camera features transformed into the BEV Polar domain.

papers.title: NTU4DRadLM: 4D Radar-centric Multi-Modal Dataset for Localization and Mapping
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of multi-modal datasets for robust SLAM by releasing NTU4DRadLM, the first 6-sensor dataset (including 4D radar and thermal) specifically designed for localization with ground-truth odometry and loop closures.

papers.title: Echoes Beyond Points: Unleashing the Power of Raw Radar Data in Multi-modality Fusion
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Bypasses sparse, suboptimal point clouds by fusing rich, lossless distance and speed features from the raw radar spectrum directly with image features in Bird's-Eye View (BEV).

papers.title: The Oxford Radar RobotCar Dataset: A Radar Extension to the Oxford RobotCar Dataset
papers.summary: Extends the popular Oxford RobotCar dataset with a high-resolution scanning radar to provide the research community with a large-scale, multi-sensor dataset for developing autonomous driving perception algorithms that are robust to adverse weather.

papers.title: GT-MilliNoise: Graph transformer for point-wise denoising of indoor millimetre-wave point clouds
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of denoising sparse and noisy mmWave point clouds by introducing GT-MilliNoise, a graph-based transformer architecture that utilizes specialized temporal and geometric blocks to effectively capture dynamic behavior and local structures, achieving a 5% accuracy gain over state-of-the-art methods.

papers.title: SDDiff: Boost Radar Perception via Spatial-Doppler Diffusion
papers.summary: Addresses the bias arising from treating point cloud extraction and ego-velocity estimation independently by proposing a Spatial-Doppler Diffusion model that employs directional diffusion and iterative refinement to simultaneously optimize both tasks.

papers.title: RaLD: Generating High-Resolution 3D Radar Point Clouds with Latent Diffusion
papers.summary: Generates high-resolution 3D radar point clouds from low-resolution inputs using a latent diffusion model that captures complex spatial structures and noise characteristics of radar data.

papers.title: CaRLi-V: Camera-RADAR-LiDAR Point-Wise 3D Velocity Estimation
papers.summary: Estimates dense 3D point-wise velocity via a closed-form solution that combines radial velocity from a novel raw radar "velocity cube," tangential velocity from optical flow, and range from LiDAR.

papers.title: RaCalNet: Radar Calibration Network for Sparse‑Supervised Metric Depth Estimation
papers.summary: Proposes a network to calibrate radar with sparse LiDAR supervision to produce metric depth estimation from radar and RGB with minimal dense supervision. :contentReference

papers.title: Triangulation of 3D target points from radar range and bearing data
