papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Solves the 3D radar triangulation problem by formulating the maximum likelihood estimation as an eigenvalue problem, allowing for the efficient extraction of the global optimum solution.

papers.title: Physics-Aware Gaussian Radar Enhancement for All-weather Perception
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Solves radar sparsity by modeling each detection as a 3D beam frustum, rather than an isolated point, and uses this physical prior to train a dual-stream network for dense point cloud enhancement.

papers.title: Radar-Mamba: 4D Millimeter-Wave Point Cloud Enhancement via State Space Models
papers.summary: Proposes a lightweight Mamba-based model that enhances sparse 4D radar data by efficiently capturing spatial-temporal features and integrating Doppler information, using LiDAR for supervision.

papers.title: RLCNet: A Novel Deep Feature-Matching-Based Method for Online Target-Free Radar-LiDAR Calibration
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Tackles radar-LiDAR extrinsic calibration by using a deep network that first establishes sparse keypoint matches and then refines them into dense correspondences to overcome radar point cloud sparsity.

papers.title: RadarSplat: Radar Gaussian Splatting for High-Fidelity Data Synthesis and 3D Reconstruction of Autonomous Driving Scenes
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Overcomes the poor performance of neural representations in noisy radar scenarios by integrating Gaussian Splatting with a novel noise model, enabling high-fidelity 3D reconstruction and realistic data synthesis.

papers.title: NeuRadar: Neural Radiance Fields for Automotive Radar Point Clouds
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Pioneers the use of Neural Radiance Fields for radar by proposing a model that jointly generates multi-sensor data and uses a probabilistic representation to accurately capture the stochastic nature of radar point clouds.

papers.title: MilliNoise: a Millimeter-wave Radar Sparse Point Cloud Dataset in Indoor Scenarios
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of labeled training data for radar denoising by releasing MilliNoise, a large dataset where a motion capture system is used to accurately label every sparse point as "true" or "noise".

papers.title: RMap: Millimeter-Wave Radar Mapping Through Volumetric Upsampling
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Addresses the severe sparsity and noise of radar data for 3D mapping by using a generative transformer to upsample, denoise, and fill in the point cloud to resemble a lidar map.

papers.title: RaViDeep: Target Detection Based on Deep Fusion of Radar and Vision in Berthing Scenarios
papers.summary: Solves radar sparsity and image depth-deficiency by first using image semantics to densify radar points, and then using radar's accurate depth to guide image depth estimation, fusing both enhanced results for detection.

papers.title: A Recurrent CNN for Online Object Detection on Raw Radar Frames
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
papers.summary: Addresses the non-causal and computationally expensive nature of 3D convolution-based detectors by proposing a recurrent (ConvLSTM) architecture that enables efficient, causal (real-time) online object detection.

papers.title: MmWave Radar Point Cloud Segmentation using GMM in Multimodal Traffic Monitoring
papers.summary: Solves the challenge of sparse data in radar traffic monitoring by using a high-resolution mmWave sensor and applying an unsupervised Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) to a novel feature vector for point cloud segmentation.

papers.title: A Multi-Target Action Recognition Method Based on UWB MIMO Radar Space-Time-Frequency Fusion Features
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Addresses the feature aliasing problem in multi-target Human Action Recognition (HAR) by proposing a space-time-frequency fusion framework using UWB MIMO radar.  This method performs 2D imaging to physically separate multiple targets, followed by multi-height 3D imaging to extract independent micro-Doppler features for each subject. These fused features are then processed by a lightweight Spatio-Temporal Decomposed Network (STD-Net), achieving superior accuracy, real-time performance, and significantly reduced Doppler cross-talk in real-world environments.

papers.title: Sparse Spike Encoding of Channel Responses for Energy Efficient Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses edge-device energy constraints in Human Activity Recognition by proposing a Spiking Convolutional Autoencoder (SCAE) jointly trained with an SNN.  It directly encodes Channel Impulse Responses into sparse spike trains, eliminating Doppler preprocessing while achieving a ~96% F1 score and 81.1% sparsity for high energy efficiency.

papers.title: WLMMF-Wireless Multimodal Fusion of mm-Wave Radar and Vision for Real-Time Human Action Recognition (RT-HAR) in Mobile Robotics (MR)
papers.summary: Addresses the need for robust Human Action Recognition in mobile robotics by proposing WLMMF, a wireless-aware multimodal fusion framework integrating mmWave radar and vision.  It uniquely embeds channel-state feedback into the fusion process for real-time adaptability, achieving 92% accuracy with sub-50ms latency

papers.title: LWDSC-Net: A Lightweight Wavelet Dual-Branch Coupling Network for mmWave Radar-Based Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses the computational constraints of mmWave radar HAR on edge devices by proposing LWDSC-Net, a lightweight dual-branch network that embeds a 1-D Haar wavelet transform to decouple signals into low and high frequencies. It utilizes a LightTCN for global dependencies and a Wavelet-Domain Depthwise Separable Convolution (WD-DSC) for micromotion features, achieving 98.76% accuracy with ultra-low latency on NVIDIA Jetson Nano.

papers.title: Activity-dependent resolution adjustment for radar-based human activity recognition
papers.summary: Enhances radar-based human activity recognition (HAR) by replacing fixed-resolution spectrograms with an adaptive time-frequency representation that nonlinearly adjusts resolution based on activity intensity, thereby capturing critical micro-Doppler signatures more effectively.

papers.title: mmWEAVER: Environment-Specific mmWave Signal Synthesis from a Photo and Activity Description
papers.summary: Overcomes the computational cost and complexity of physical mmWave simulations by introducing mmWeaver, a framework that uses Implicit Neural Representations (INRs) and hypernetworks conditioned on environmental (RGB-D) and motion (text-to-pose) priors to synthesize high-fidelity, environment-specific complex mmWave signals, significantly boosting downstream task performance while operating 6-35x faster than simulations.

papers.title: M4Human: A Large-Scale Multimodal mmWave Radar Benchmark for Human Mesh Reconstruction
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of vision-based human sensing (occlusion, privacy) and existing radar datasets (sparse labels, limited scale) by introducing M4Human, the largest multimodal benchmark (661K frames) featuring high-resolution mmWave radar, RGB-D data, and high-quality MoCap annotations for robust human mesh reconstruction.

papers.title: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset and Benchmark for Human Action Recognition, Understanding and Reasoning (CUHK-X)
papers.summary: Addresses the inability of current LLMs/LVLMs to handle non-RGB modalities and fine-grained action reasoning by introducing CUHK-X, a large-scale multimodal dataset (RGB, Depth, IMU, mmWave) with logically consistent, prompt-generated captions that benchmarks recognition, understanding, and reasoning tasks.

papers.title: mmExpert: Integrating Large Language Models for Comprehensive mmWave Data Synthesis and Understanding
papers.summary: Overcomes the high cost of data acquisition and annotation in mmWave sensing by employing Large Language Models to power a synthetic data generation flywheel, enabling the training of models capable of zero-shot generalization in real-world environments.

papers.title: OrganicHAR: Towards Activity Discovery in Organic Settings for Privacy Preserving Sensors Using Efficient Video Analysis
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Introduces OrganicHAR, a framework that combines privacy-preserving sensors with efficient video analysis to discover human activities in organic settings while ensuring user privacy.

papers.title: OG-PCL: Efficient Sparse Point Cloud Processing for Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Proposes a lightweight parallel network (OG-PCL) that introduces an "Occupancy-Gated Convolution" (OGConv) block to provide an explicit compensation mechanism for handling sparse radar point clouds.

papers.title: OctoNet: A Large-Scale Multi-Modal Dataset for Human Activity Understanding Grounded in Motion-Captured 3D Pose Labels
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Fosters multi-modal human activity research by releasing a massive dataset (OctoNet) that uniquely uses motion-captured 3D pose labels as the high-fidelity ground truth for all 12 diverse sensor modalities.

papers.title: mmHSense: Multi-Modal and Distributed mmWave ISAC Datasets for Human Sensing
papers.summary: Releases a multi-modal mmWave ISAC dataset and demonstrates using parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) to adapt a single model to diverse human sensing tasks efficiently.

papers.title: Robust Cross-Domain RF-Based Multimodal Activity Recognition with Few-Shot Adaptation
papers.summary: Solves poor cross-domain performance in multimodal RF (RFID, WiFi, mmWave) sensing by using a hybrid feature/decision fusion framework that dynamically weights each modality's contribution to enable rapid few-shot adaptation.

papers.title: Neural-HAR: A Dimension-Gated CNN Accelerator for Real-Time Radar Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Solves the high computational cost of radar HAR on edge devices by proposing a parameter-efficient CNN (GateCNN) that uses dual-path gated convolutions to modulate Doppler features with temporal gates.

papers.title: Human Activity Recognition Based on Multipath Fusion in Non-line-of-sight Corner
papers.summary: Achieves NLOS activity recognition by fusing multipath radar signals that bounce around corners to sense hidden human activities.

papers.title: Enhancing Activity Recognition: Motion Waveform Preprocessing from Millimeter-Wave Radar Data for Transformer-Based Classification
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Multimedia and Expo Workshops (ICMEW)
papers.summary: Improves transformer-based activity recognition by designing specialized preprocessing that extracts motion waveforms from mmWave radar signals.

papers.title: Resolution-Adaptive Micro-Doppler Spectrogram for Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Enhances HAR performance by adaptively adjusting micro-Doppler spectrogram resolution based on activity characteristics and computational constraints.

papers.title: A Novel Multimodal LLM-Driven RF Sensing Method for Human Activity Recognition
papers.publication: International Conference on Microwave, Antennas & Circuits (ICMAC)
papers.summary: Leverages large language models to interpret multimodal RF sensing data by bridging radar signals with semantic understanding for improved activity recognition.

papers.title: RadMamba: Efficient Human Activity Recognition through Radar-based Micro-Doppler-Oriented Mamba State-Space Model
papers.summary: Achieves efficient HAR using Mamba state-space models that capture micro-Doppler temporal dependencies with linear complexity for real-time processing.

papers.title: DGAR: A Unified Domain Generalization Framework for RF-Based Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses cross-domain HAR challenges through unified domain generalization framework that learns domain-invariant radar representations.

papers.title: RadProPoser: Uncertainty-Aware Human Pose Estimation and Activity Classification from Raw Radar Data
papers.summary: Jointly performs pose estimation and activity classification from raw radar with uncertainty quantification to improve reliability in ambiguous scenarios.

papers.title: RadHARSimulator V1: Model-Based FMCW Radar Human Activity Recognition Simulator
papers.summary: Overcomes the challenge of acquiring diverse, high-fidelity radar data for Human Activity Recognition (HAR) by developing a model-based simulator that generates realistic micro-Doppler signatures for various activities.

papers.title: Multi-Head Adaptive Graph Convolution Network for Sparse Point Cloud-Based Human Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses the limitation of fixed graph kernels for sparse point clouds by proposing a multi-head module that generates multiple, dynamic kernels, each adapting to different aspects of the local neighborhood's geometry.

papers.title: Few-Shot Adaptation to Unseen Conditions for Wireless-Based Human Activity Recognition Without Fine-Tuning
papers.summary: Eliminates the need for fine-tuning in wireless activity recognition by using meta-learning to learn a condition-invariant latent space, where recognition is performed based on activity similarity rather than a condition-dependent mapping.

papers.title: Hood: Real-time human presence and out-of-distribution detection using fmcw radar
papers.summary: Simultaneously tackles human presence and out-of-distribution detection by using a reconstruction-based architecture on radar images to differentiate humans-with-clutter from clutter-only scenes.

papers.title: Advanced Millimeter-Wave Radar System for Real-Time Multiple-Human Tracking and Fall Detection
papers.summary: Proposes a three-radar fusion framework that uses SNR-based dynamic DBSCAN clustering and a probability matrix to achieve robust multi-person tracking and fall detection.

papers.title: 4D radar simulator for human activity recognition
papers.summary: Overcomes the high cost of prototyping 4D radar for HAR by building an end-to-end simulator that uses a motion capture dataset to generate point clouds for algorithm benchmarking.

papers.title: A Dataset for Multi-intensity Continuous Human Activity Recognition through Passive Sensing
papers.summary: Solves the failure of mmWave radar to capture micro-scale activities (like typing) by using an adaptive Doppler resolution processing pipeline to enhance signal precision.

papers.title: Fast Human Action Recognition via Millimeter Wave Radar Point Cloud Sequences Learning
papers.publication: ACM International Conference on Information and Knowledge Management
papers.summary: Solves the problem of processing sparse radar point clouds on resource-constrained devices by using a two-stage feature extractor that first builds local graphs with self-attention (to handle sparsity) and then uses 3D convolution to reduce sequence length before temporal modeling.

papers.title: XRF55: A Radio Frequency Dataset for Human Indoor Action Analysis
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of diverse, large-scale RF datasets by releasing XRF55, which uniquely combines three distinct RF modalities (RFID, Wi-Fi, mmWave) and Kinect ground truth across 55 action classes

papers.title: Attention-Refined Unrolling for Sparse Sequential Micro-Doppler Reconstruction
papers.publication: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
papers.summary: Solves the problem of slow and inaccurate micro-Doppler reconstruction from highly sparse JCS measurements by combining a single unrolled hard-thresholding layer with an attention mechanism for refinement.

papers.title: CubeLearn: End-to-end Learning for Human Motion Recognition from Raw mmWave Radar Signals
papers.summary: Replaces the conventional, fixed DFT preprocessing in radar-based motion recognition with a learnable, end-to-end module to extract task-optimized features directly from raw signals, boosting performance especially for lightweight models.

papers.title: MiliPoint: a point cloud dataset for mmWave radar
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Fosters research into point-based deep learning for radar by releasing a large-scale, diverse dataset for human activity recognition, complete with established performance baselines.

papers.title: MM-Fi: multi-modal non-intrusive 4D human dataset for versatile wireless sensing
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Provides a large-scale multi-modal dataset combining 4D radar, RGB-D, and motion capture data to facilitate research in wireless human sensing.

papers.title: On Edge Human Action Recognition Using Radar-Based Sensing and Deep Learning
papers.summary: Achieves real-time human action recognition on an edge device by feeding radar Range-Doppler maps into a deep neural network, specifically evaluating the accuracy and computational cost tradeoff for deployment.

papers.title: Hand Gesture Recognition from Doppler Radar Signals Using Echo State Networks
papers.summary: Addresses the high computational costs of conventional deep learning in radar-based Hand Gesture Recognition (HGR) by proposing an Echo State Network (ESN) approach.  It processes range-time and Doppler-time feature maps from FMCW radar signals through parallel RNN-based reservoirs and readout classifiers (e.g., SVM, random forests), outperforming existing models on the Soli and Dop-NET datasets while maintaining low computational overhead for resource-constrained environments.

papers.title: MTxLSTM: Multi-Task Learning for Gesture Recognition and Person Identification using a Miniature Radar Sensor
papers.summary: English: Addresses the computational inefficiency of separate models for radar-based HCI by introducing MTxLSTM, a multi-task learning framework that simultaneously performs gesture recognition and person identification using a miniature radar. By integrating CNNs with xLSTM (marking its first application in radar sensing) to fuse micro-Doppler and range-Doppler features, the model achieves superior accuracy (99.21% for gestures, 98.59% for identification) while maintaining moderate complexity.

papers.title: Fine-Grained Radar Hand Gesture Recognition Method Based on Variable-Channel DRSN
papers.summary: Enables continuous, free-form aerial handwriting recognition by introducing mmScribe, a streaming end-to-end system that utilizes FMCW mmWave radar and a hybrid CTC/attention architecture to decode unsegmented hand movements into text. The system features a dynamic block training strategy for arbitrary length input and a two-stage decoding process (CTC for streaming character hypotheses, Attention for text-level rescoring), achieving excellent performance and low latency on mobile devices.

papers.title: Domain-Generalized Gesture Recognition via mmWave Radar Signal Multiview Learning
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of single-view and simplistic fusion methods in mmWave gesture recognition by proposing a domain-generalized multiview learning framework. This approach integrates domain knowledge with Dempster-Shafer (DS) evidence theory for causal feature extraction and fusion, achieving superior out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization across diverse users and scenarios.

papers.title: Mmscribe: Streaming End-to-End Aerial Handwriting Text Translation Via Mmwave Radar
papers.summary: Introduces mmScribe, the first end-to-end system for real-time aerial handwriting recognition using mmWave radar, combining a novel radar processing pipeline with a transformer-based model to achieve 92.1% accuracy in streaming text translation.

papers.title: mmWaveTryOn: The first mmWave-RGB Dataset for Clothes Try-On Multimodal Gesture Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses the privacy and lighting limitations of RGB-based smart mirrors by introducing "mmWaveTryOn," the first multimodal dataset designed to benchmark mmWave-based gesture recognition for virtual clothes try-on scenarios.

papers.title: Millimeter-Wave Gesture Recognition in ISAC:Does Reducing Sensing Airtime Hamper Accuracy?
papers.summary: Quantifies the trade-off between sensing and communication airtime in mmWave ISAC, demonstrating that reducing sensing airtime to 25% results in a negligible 0.15% drop in CNN-based gesture recognition accuracy, thereby validating efficient resource sharing.

papers.title: RF-Behavior: A Multimodal Radio-Frequency Dataset for Human Behavior and Emotion Analysis
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of a comprehensive RF dataset by releasing RF-Behavior, which uniquely spans gestures, activities, and emotions using 13 radars and RFID, all grounded by precise motion-capture.

papers.title: Real-Time Human–Drone Interaction via Active Multimodal Gesture Recognition Under Limited Field of View in Indoor Environments
papers.summary: Overcomes sensor limitations (lighting, FoV) in drone gesture control by combining a lightweight image/point-cloud fusion network with an active perception controller that optimizes the sensor's FoV.

papers.title: Simulation-Based Radar Gesture Recognition Using Domain Adversarial Training
papers.summary: Overcomes the lack of real radar data for gesture recognition by generating synthetic data with Blender/ray-tracing and using Domain-Adversarial training to bridge the sim-to-real gap.

papers.title: Venus: Generating Large-scale mmWave Radar Data via Few 2D Videos for Gesture Recognition While Lying Down
papers.summary: Solves the data scarcity problem for radar-based gesture recognition in lying postures by first using a spatio-temporal diffusion model to generate diverse gesture sequences and then a meta-learning network to adapt these into realistic radar signals, all from a few 2D videos.

papers.title: An Explainable Tiny-Fast Kolmogorov–Arnold Network for Gesture-Based Air Handwriting Recognition of Tifinagh Letters in Resource-Constrained IoT Device
papers.summary: Fills a cultural and technical gap by creating the first real-time, air-handwriting recognition system for Tifinagh letters, using a novel, interpretable, and tiny Kolmogorov–Arnold Network (XTiny-FastKAN) designed for resource-constrained IoT devices.

papers.title: mmWave Radar-based Unsupervised Gesture Recognition via Image-Aligned Heterogeneous Domain Transfer
papers.summary: Achieves unsupervised gesture recognition by aligning radar spectrograms with image features through heterogeneous domain transfer learning.

papers.title: mmPencil: Toward Writing-Style-Independent In-Air Handwriting Recognition via mmWave Radar and Large Vision-Language Model
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Enables writing-style-independent in-air handwriting recognition by combining mmWave radar with vision-language models for semantic understanding.

papers.title: Human-Centered Fully Adaptive Radar for Gesture Recognition in Smart Environments
papers.summary: Develops fully adaptive radar that automatically adjusts parameters based on user behavior and environment for robust gesture recognition.

papers.title: mmEgoHand: Egocentric Hand Pose Estimation and Gesture Recognition with Head-mounted Millimeter-wave Radar and IMU
papers.summary: Achieves egocentric hand tracking by fusing head-mounted mmWave radar with IMU for VR/AR applications with minimal occlusion.

papers.title: mmDigit: A Real-Time Digit Recognition Framework in Air-Writing Using FMCW Radar
papers.summary: Enables real-time in-air digit writing recognition using FMCW radar by tracking hand motion trajectories and extracting stroke patterns.

papers.title: mmHand: Toward Pixel-Level-Accuracy Hand Localization Using a Single Commodity mmWave Device
papers.summary: Achieves pixel-level hand localization accuracy from single mmWave device by combining advanced signal processing with learning-based refinement.

papers.title: Hand Gesture Recognition With Uncertainty Awareness via FMCW Radar Sensing and Deep Learning
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of reliability in deep learning-based gesture recognition by introducing a model that quantifies its own prediction uncertainty using a combination of techniques like Monte Carlo dropout and deep ensembles.

papers.title: Rodar: Robust Gesture Recognition Based on mmWave Radar Under Human Activity Interference
papers.summary: Achieves robust gesture recognition under activity interference by disentangling hand gestures from body movements using multi-view deformable attention.

papers.title: Eat-Radar: Continuous Fine-Grained Intake Gesture Detection Using FMCW Radar and 3D Temporal Convolutional Network with Attention
papers.publication: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
papers.summary: Achieves fine-grained, continuous detection of eating and drinking gestures by applying a 3D temporal convolutional network with attention to radar Range-Doppler data, validated on a new public dataset featuring diverse eating styles in realistic meal sessions.

papers.title: A Multimodal Video and Radar Fusion Framework for High-Accuracy Isolated Sign Language Recognition
papers.summary: Addresses inadequate multimodal fusion in sign language recognition by proposing a two-stage attention mechanism (statistical and contrastive) to deeply fuse sEMG and IMU signals by first selecting active features and then weighting them based on inter-modal information gain.

papers.title: GaitSADA: Self-Aligned Domain Adaptation for mmWave Gait Recognition
papers.publication: International Conference on Mobile Ad Hoc and Smart Systems (MASS)
papers.summary: Tackles the poor generalization of mmWave gait recognition by using a two-stage semi-supervised domain adaptation method that combines contrastive learning with consistency training to align spatial and temporal domain shifts.

papers.title: Towards Domain-Independent and Real-Time Gesture Recognition Using mmWave Signal
papers.summary: Achieves domain-independent, real-time gesture recognition by using a novel data augmentation framework to improve generalization and a spatial-temporal segmentation algorithm to enable continuous recognition.

papers.title: Physically-Interpretable Data Augmentation for Multi-Range Hand Gesture Recognition Using FMCW Radar Time Series
papers.summary: Bypasses the complexity of augmenting raw radar data by instead extracting and manipulating physically interpretable time series (range, angle) to simulate new gesture variations.

papers.title: Diffusion-Based Reconstruction of 3D Occupancy Maps from 4D Radar Tensors
papers.summary: Addresses the challenges of reconstructing 3D occupancy maps from 4D radar tensors by proposing a diffusion-based framework.  It integrates doppler-aware dimensionality reduction, hierarchical pillar-based representation, and a conditional diffusion model, significantly improving IoU to over 30% and reducing Chamfer distance by 4× with ~50ms latency.

papers.title: RIMCvT: Empowering Radar-Based In-vehicle Monitoring with Fine-Tuning Pre-trained Convolutional Vision Transformer
papers.summary: Addresses the fragmentation of radar-based in-vehicle monitoring systems by introducing RIMCvT, a unified framework that fine-tunes a pre-trained Convolutional vision Transformer (CvT) with a lightweight bypass module to efficiently integrate radar and audio data, achieving superior performance (92.93% average accuracy) across occupancy, gesture, drowsiness, and vital sign monitoring tasks.

papers.title: mmCounter: Static People Counting in Dense Indoor Scenarios Using mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of counting static individuals in dense indoor scenarios using mmWave radar by introducing mmCounter, a system that isolates ultra-low frequency (< 1 Hz) breathing and micro-movement signals to accurately distinguish and enumerate up to three people per square meter without prior knowledge of the crowd size.

papers.title: MMWiLoc: A Multi‑Sensor Dataset and Robust Device‑Free Localization Method Using Commercial Off‑The‐Shelf Millimeter Wave Wi‑Fi Devices
papers.summary: Introduces a synchronized multi‑sensor dataset (mmWave WiFi + 2.4 GHz WiFi + radar) and a device‑free localization method achieving centimetre‐level accuracy using commercial mmWave WiFi.

papers.title: P2MFDS: A Privacy‑Preserving Multimodal Fall Detection System for Elderly People in Bathroom Environments
papers.summary: Addresses fall‑detection for elderly in bathrooms by fusing multiple sensor modalities in a privacy‑preserving way to reliably detect falls in sensitive environments.

papers.title: Exploration of Low-Cost but Accurate Radar-Based Human Motion Direction Determination
papers.summary: Achieves accurate motion direction estimation with low-cost radar by developing efficient algorithms that maximize information extraction from limited hardware.

papers.title: End-to-End Radar Human Segmentation with Differentiable Positional Encoding
papers.summary: Performs end-to-end human segmentation from radar by introducing differentiable positional encodings that adapt to irregular radar point distributions.

papers.title: MVDoppler-Pose: Multi-Modal Multi-View mmWave Sensing for Long-Distance Self-Occluded Human Walking Pose Estimation
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Estimates human poses at long distances under self-occlusion by fusing multi-view mmWave Doppler signatures across multiple radar perspectives.

papers.title: SelaFD:Seamless Adaptation of Vision Transformer Fine-tuning for Radar-based Human Activity Recognition
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
papers.summary: Seamlessly adapts pre-trained vision transformers to radar-based HAR through selective fine-tuning strategies that preserve learned representations.

papers.title: BSENSE: In-vehicle Child Detection and Vital Sign Monitoring with a Single mmWave Radar and Synthetic Reflectors
papers.summary: Detects in-vehicle child presence and monitors vital signs using synthetic reflectors to enhance radar signal coverage in confined cabin spaces.

papers.title: RadarOcc: Robust 3D Occupancy Prediction with 4D Imaging Radar
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Bypasses sparse radar point clouds by processing the full 4D radar tensor directly, using sidelobe-aware sparsification and spherical encoding to manage noise and volume for 3D occupancy prediction.

papers.title: MMVR: Millimeter-Wave Multi-view Radar Dataset and Benchmark for Indoor Perception
papers.summary: Addresses the scarcity of diverse, high-resolution indoor radar data by releasing a large-scale, multi-view, multi-room dataset (MMVR) with comprehensive annotations for benchmarking key perception tasks.

papers.title: Emergency Response Person Localization and Vital Sign Estimation Using a Semi-Autonomous Robot Mounted SFCW Radar
papers.summary: Proposes a complete processing chain (using 2D-MUSIC) for a robot-mounted through-wall radar to detect, localize, and estimate vital signs, validating it on a new, challenging benchmark dataset.

papers.title: RDGait: A mmWave Based Gait User Recognition System for Complex Indoor Environments Using Single-chip Radar
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Solves multipath ghost interference in complex indoor environments by using a novel, prior-free ghost detection algorithm based only on velocity change patterns.

papers.title: Human Detection from 4D Radar Data in Low-Visibility Field Conditions
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Improves human detection in low-visibility conditions by introducing a multi-view radar semantic segmentation (MVRSS) dataset and a novel architecture that fuses multi-view radar data for robust detection.

papers.title: mmFall: Fall Detection using 4D MmWave Radar and a Hybrid Variational RNN AutoEncoder
papers.summary: DCircumvents the need for fall data collection by training a variational RNN autoencoder in a semi-supervised manner on only normal activities, detecting falls as anomalies in the radar signal.

papers.title: A Global-Local Network for Human Pose Estimation with Completed mmWave Radar Point Clouds
papers.summary: Addresses the sparsity and incompleteness of mmWave radar point clouds in human pose estimation by proposing a Global-Local Network. This two-stage framework first employs a Point Cloud Completion (PCC) network with semi-synthetic supervision to densify the data, and then utilizes a dual-branch architecture to fuse global structural context from the completed clouds with local fine-grained details (velocity, intensity) from the original sparse points.

papers.title: milliMamba: Specular-Aware Human Pose Estimation via Dual mmWave Radar with Multi-Frame Mamba Fusion
papers.summary: Overcomes the signal sparsity caused by specular reflection in radar-based Human Pose Estimation by introducing milliMamba, a framework utilizing a Cross-View Fusion Mamba encoder and Spatio-Temporal-Cross Attention decoder to efficiently model long-term dependencies and infer missing joints, achieving significant accuracy gains over state-of-the-art baselines.

papers.title: Vomee: A Multimodal Sensing Platform for Video, Audio, mmWave and Skeleton Data Capturing
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of open-source solutions for time-aligned multi-sensor recording by introducing Vomee, a multimodal platform that captures synchronized video, audio, mmWave, and skeleton data with hardware-level interference avoidance.

papers.title: RAPTR: Radar-based 3D Pose Estimation using Transformer
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Overcomes the high cost of 3D labels by proposing a Transformer model that uses weak supervision (2D keypoints and 3D BBoxes) and a two-stage decoder with pseudo-3D deformable attention for pose estimation.

papers.title: Few-shot Human Motion Recognition through Multi-Aspect mmWave FMCW Radar Data
papers.summary: Improves radar-inertial odometry for drone navigation in GNSS-denied environments by incorporating a barometer and using a robust m-estimator to more adaptively handle sensor outliers than binary filters.

papers.title: Learning to Analyze Human Skeletal by Radar–Camera Supervision
papers.publication: Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
papers.summary: Learns radar-based skeleton estimation through camera supervision that provides ground truth skeletal annotations during training.

papers.title: RadarLLM: Empowering Large Language Models to Understand Human Motion from Millimeter-wave Point Cloud Sequence
papers.publication: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
papers.summary: Empowers LLMs to interpret human motion from radar point clouds by developing specialized encoders and language-aligned representations.

papers.title: mmPose-FK: A forward kinematics approach to dynamic skeletal pose estimation using mmWave radars
papers.summary: Addresses the instability (vibration) of joint estimations from noisy radar by integrating a forward kinematics (FK) mechanism directly into the deep learning model to enforce temporal consistency.

papers.title: Diffusion Model Is a Good Pose Estimator from 3D RF-Vision
papers.summary: Solves inaccurate and inconsistent pose estimation from noisy radar by using a diffusion model that is guided by dedicated body-part feature extractors and structural priors to overcome miss-detections.

papers.title: SUPER: Seated Upper Body Pose Estimation using mmWave Radars
papers.summary: Solves the conflicting radar signature problem (e.g., high-motion arms vs. low-motion torso) in seated pose estimation by using a dual-radar fusion algorithm to create complementary point clouds.

papers.title: mmHPE: Robust Multiscale 3-D Human Pose Estimation Using a Single mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Improves 3D pose estimation accuracy from sparse radar point clouds by introducing a multiscale feature extraction network that captures both local and global body structures.

papers.title: QRFPose: Query-Based 3D Pose Estimation using Radio Signals
papers.publication: International Conference on Wireless Communications and Signal Processing (WCSP)
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of divide-and-conquer methods by treating body joints as learnable queries that use deformable attention to adaptively aggregate features from sparse global and dense local RF regions.

papers.title: Robust Human Detection under Visual Degradation via Thermal and mmWave Radar Fusion
papers.summary: Improves human detection in visually degraded conditions by fusing thermal and radar data using a novel uncertainty-guided method, which leverages Bayesian feature extraction to mitigate sensor-specific noise.

papers.title: Hawkeye: Hectometer-range Subcentimeter Localization for Large-scale mmWave Backscatter
papers.summary: Achieves large-scale, sub-centimeter 3D localization by using a Van Atta array tag (to suppress multipath) and a novel algorithm that leverages spectral leakage for fine-grained positioning.

papers.title: mmSignature: Semi-supervised human identification system based on millimeter wave radar
papers.summary: Solves the high cost of manual data labeling by using a semi-supervised co-training framework that leverages the complementary information between radar point clouds and range-velocity maps to learn from unlabeled data.

papers.title: mmPose-NLP: A Natural Language Processing Approach to Precise Skeletal Pose Estimation Using mmWave Radars
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
papers.summary: Pioneers the estimation of a large number of skeletal key points from radar by uniquely framing the problem as a natural language processing task, where a sequence-to-sequence model interprets voxelized point clouds like text.

papers.title: mmBody Benchmark: 3D Body Reconstruction Dataset and Analysis for Millimeter Wave Radar
papers.summary: Establishes a comprehensive benchmark for 3D body reconstruction by creating a large-scale dataset to systematically compare radar and cameras, revealing radar's superior accuracy to RGB but inferiority to depth under normal conditions, and its greater robustness in adverse weather.

papers.title: mRI: Multi-modal 3D Human Pose Estimation Dataset using mmWave, RGB-D, and Inertial Sensors
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Fills the gap in home-based health monitoring research by releasing a large-scale, multi-modal dataset (mRI) of rehabilitation exercises captured with synchronized mmWave, RGB-D, and inertial sensors.

papers.title: Fast and scalable human pose estimation using mmWave point cloud
papers.summary: Tackles the sparsity and data scarcity issues in mmWave pose estimation by combining a multi-frame representation with a meta-learning framework to enable rapid adaptation to unseen scenarios.

papers.title: Toward Continuous and Contactless Cardiac Monitoring: A State-Space Approach With FMCW Radar
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of contact-based ECG by proposing a continuous and contactless cardiac monitoring system using FMCW radar. The system models the heart as a signal generator and the radar as a state observer, utilizing reinforcement learning to optimize state-space matrices for accurate ECG reconstruction. It achieves clinical-grade accuracy (PCC ~0.9, HRV MAE 16.45 ms) on overnight sleep data, overcoming generalization challenges with minimal training.

papers.title: Robust HRV Monitoring via Massive Radio Sensing
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Addresses the poor generalization of radio-based HRV monitoring in complex environments by introducing a "Massive Radio Sensing" framework that leverages large-scale, environment-variant sampling and structured deep learning to essentially average out environmental noise and achieve robust estimation.

papers.title: Hankel Dynamic Mode Decomposition for Radar-Based Respiratory Sensing and Tracking
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: Overcomes the failure of traditional processing in cluttered, non-stationary environments by employing Hankel Dynamic Mode Decomposition (HDMD) to treat noisy radar data as a dynamical system, robustly isolating respiratory oscillatory modes.

papers.title: Radar-APLANC: Unsupervised Radar-based Heartbeat Sensing via Augmented Pseudo-Label and Noise Contrast
papers.summary: Solves the need for costly labeled data in radar heartbeat sensing by using an unsupervised, noise-contrastive framework that generates augmented pseudo-labels from the radar matrix itself.

papers.title: Remote Emotion Recognition Using Continuous-Wave Bio-Radar System
papers.summary: Demonstrates that machine learning on vital signs from a non-contact Bio-Radar can match the emotion recognition accuracy of certified contact-based systems, enabling monitoring without subject awareness.

papers.title: CogPhys: Assessing Cognitive Load via Multimodal Remote and Contact-based Physiological Sensing
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: English: Addresses the lack of data for remote cognitive load monitoring by introducing a large, multi-modal dataset (video, thermal, RF) and demonstrating that fusing remote physiological signals can achieve classification accuracy comparable to intrusive, contact-based sensors.

papers.title: LifWavNet: Lifting Wavelet-based Network for Non-contact ECG Reconstruction from Radar
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of fixed wavelet methods by using a learnable lifting wavelet network and a multi-resolution STFT loss to adaptively synthesize ECG waveforms from radar signals.

papers.title: Evidential Remote Physiological Measurement via Uncertainty-aware Fusion of Video and RF
papers.summary: olves the problem of suboptimal video-RF fusion by training each modality to predict its own uncertainty, enabling a dynamic fusion that intelligently weights each sensor based on its real-time reliability.

papers.title: FusionPhys: A Flexible Framework for Fusing Complementary Sensing Modalities in Remote Physiological Measurement
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
papers.summary: Overcomes the limitations of single-sensor (e.g., camera) physiological measurement by proposing a framework that adaptively fuses heterogeneous modalities (visible, NIR, radar) based on their shared representation as time-varying signals.

papers.title: From High-SNR Radar Signal to ECG: A Transfer Learning Model with Cardio-Focusing Algorithm for Scenarios with Limited Data
papers.summary: Tackles data scarcity in radar-based ECG recovery by using a transfer learning model that pre-trains on unlabeled radar data and only requires a few paired samples for fine-tuning, complemented by an algorithm that actively tracks the heart to ensure high-quality signal acquisition.

papers.title: Simulation for Noncontact Radar-Based Physiological Sensing Using Depth-Camera-Derived Human 3D Model with Electromagnetic Scattering Analysis
papers.summary: Enhances the realism of radar-based physiological simulations by replacing simplified geometric models with actual human body shape and motion data captured by a depth camera.

papers.title: Enhanced sparse optimization approach for vital signal extraction from millimeter-wave radar
papers.summary: Improves vital sign extraction from radar by using a sparse optimization framework based on the Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), whose energy compaction properties enable better separation of weak and spectrally overlapping signals.

papers.title: ReBP: Short-Term Blood Pressure Estimation by Reconstructing PPG Signals Based on mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Estimates blood pressure by reconstructing PPG-like signals from mmWave radar that capture cardiovascular pulse wave characteristics.

papers.title: Atrial Fibrillation Detection via Contactless Radio Monitoring and Knowledge Transfer
papers.summary: Detects atrial fibrillation from contactless radar monitoring by transferring knowledge from clinical ECG data to radar cardiac patterns.

papers.title: Hierarchical and Multimodal Data for Daily Activity Understanding
papers.summary: Provides hierarchical multimodal dataset capturing daily activities with synchronized radar, video, and sensor data for comprehensive activity understanding.

papers.title: CardiacMamba: A Multimodal RGB-RF Fusion Framework with State Space Models for Remote Physiological Measurement
papers.summary: Measures remote physiological signals by fusing RGB and RF modalities using Mamba models that efficiently capture temporal cardiac dynamics.

papers.title: RadEye: Tracking Eye Motion Using FMCW Radar
papers.publication: Proceedings of the 2025 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems
papers.summary: Tracks eye motion using FMCW radar by detecting minute movements through high-resolution Doppler analysis for non-contact gaze estimation.

papers.title: Realistic Facial Expression Reconstruction Using Millimeter Wave
papers.summary: Reconstructs realistic facial expressions from mmWave radar by learning mappings between radar signatures and detailed facial muscle movements.

papers.title: A Heart Rate Measurement Model Based on Koopman Predictors With FMCW Radar
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Introduces a Koopman theory-based deep model that improves radar heart rate measurement by explicitly separating and processing the signal's time-invariant and time-variant dynamics.

papers.title: AirECG: Contactless Electrocardiogram for Cardiac Disease Monitoring via mmWave Sensing and Cross-domain Diffusion Model
papers.summary: Enables accurate, contactless ECG reconstruction for cardiac patients by using a cross-domain diffusion model, whose iterative denoising process can synthesize abnormal waveforms, and a guidance mechanism to ensure high fidelity.

papers.title: radarODE: An ODE-Embedded Deep Learning Model for Contactless ECG Reconstruction from Millimeter-Wave Radar
papers.summary: Improves radar-to-ECG reconstruction by embedding an Ordinary Differential Equation (ODE) into the deep learning decoder, which provides a morphological prior of the ECG waveform to guide the model and enhance robustness against body movements.

papers.title: radarODE-MTL: A Multi-Task Learning Framework with Eccentric Gradient Alignment for Robust Radar-Based ECG Reconstruction
papers.summary: Improves the robustness of radar-based ECG reconstruction against noise by deconstructing the problem into a multi-task learning framework and using a novel gradient alignment strategy to manage conflicting optimization goals.

papers.title: Comprehensive mm-Wave FMCW Radar Dataset for Vital Sign Monitoring: Embracing Extreme Physiological Scenarios
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of diverse vital sign data by releasing the first mm-Wave radar dataset validated against a Polar H10, specifically including extreme physiological scenarios and participants with unique respiratory profiles.

papers.title: Wave Dynamic Time Warping Algorithm for Periodic Signal Similarity Estimation
papers.summary: Solves the poor alignment of periodic signals by proposing a segment-based DTW algorithm that uses a 2D feature vector (amplitude and phase) to align wave segments instead of the entire sequence.

papers.title: Transforming Cardiovascular Health: a Transformer-Based Approach to Continuous, Non-Invasive Blood Pressure Estimation via Radar Sensing
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
papers.summary: Achieves medically-certified (BHS/AAMI) blood pressure estimation by using a Transformer model to leverage the predictive power of historic radar-derived pressure wave information.

papers.title: Machine Learning for Healthcare Radars: Recent Progresses in Human Vital Sign Measurement and Activity Recognition
papers.summary: Surveys the use of machine learning in radar-based healthcare, highlighting its role in enhancing traditional vital sign algorithms versus its foundational necessity for activity recognition.

papers.title: Equitable Plethysmography Blending Camera and 77 GHz Radar Sensing for Equitable, Robust Plethysmography
papers.summary: Addresses the fundamental bias of camera-based heart-rate monitoring against darker skin tones by proposing a debiasing-oriented fusion framework that integrates complementary and fairer radar data.

papers.title: Advancing sleep health equity through deep learning on large-scale nocturnal respiratory signals
papers.summary: Addresses inequitable access to sleep diagnostics by developing a robust deep learning framework that analyzes large-scale respiratory signals (including radar-derived ones via transfer learning) for scalable, remote sleep staging and apnea detection across diverse populations.

papers.title: RestAware: Non-Invasive Sleep Monitoring Using FMCW Radar and AI-Generated Summaries
papers.summary: Develops a non-invasive sleep monitoring system that uses FMCW radar for posture classification and uniquely integrates a large language model to automatically generate human-readable summaries from the sleep data.

papers.title: A Robust and Accurate FMCW MIMO Radar Vital Sign Monitoring Framework With 4-D Cardiac Beamformer and Heart-Rate Trace Carving Technique
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
papers.summary: Improves radar-based heart rate monitoring in low-SNR environments by using a 4-D beamformer to focus on the heart in both space and time-frequency, and a trace carving technique to reconstruct faint signals from noise.

papers.title: MiSleep: Human Sleep Posture Identification from Deep Learning Augmented Millimeter-wave Wireless Systems
papers.publication: ACM Transactions on Internet of Things
papers.summary: Achieves detailed sleep posture monitoring by predicting 3D body joint locations from radar, uniquely using a state machine to first detect toss-and-turn events and only performing the prediction during stable rest periods.

papers.title: CarVision: Vehicle Ranging and Tracking Using mmWave Radar for Enhanced Driver Safety
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Communications (PerCom)
papers.summary: Enhances driver safety by using mmWave radar for precise vehicle ranging and tracking to provide collision warnings and maintain safe distances.

papers.title: PhysDrive: A Multimodal Remote Physiological Measurement Dataset for In-vehicle Driver Monitoring
papers.summary: Provides multimodal dataset for driver monitoring with synchronized physiological measurements enabling research on fatigue and distraction detection.

papers.title: A Driver Activity Dataset with Multiple RGB-D Cameras and mmWave Radars
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of fine-grained, non-fatigue driver activity data by releasing a comprehensive multi-modal (radar, RGB-D) and multi-angle (face, body, hands) dataset to enable privacy-conscious research.

papers.title: mmAssist:Passive Monitoring of Driver's Attentiveness Using mmWave Sensors
papers.publication: International Conference on COMmunication Systems & NETworkS (COMSNETS)
papers.summary: Addresses the invasive nature of camera-based monitoring by using Range-Doppler information from a non-invasive mmWave radar to classify driver attentiveness levels.

papers.title: Activity recognition and user identification using mmWave radar with a shared-backbone graph network and task-specific heads
papers.summary: Addresses the underexplored area of joint activity recognition and user identification from wireless signals by proposing a dual-task graph model for mmWave FMCW radar point-cloud sequences.  It utilizes a shared graph neural backbone to extract spatial-temporal node embeddings from directed graphs, and employs task-specific heads to aggregate them for classification, achieving performance comparable to single-task baselines with low-latency inference.

papers.title: mmPPT: Hierarchical-Serialization-Enhanced Point Transformer for mmWave Pedestrian Reconstruction
papers.summary: Improves 3D pedestrian reconstruction from sparse radar by using a Point Transformer with a novel hierarchical serialization strategy that captures both fine-grained local details and global structural relationships.

papers.title: I Sense You Fast: Simultaneous Action and Identity Inference by Slimming Multi-Branch RadarNet
papers.summary: Achieves simultaneous action and identity recognition using slimmed multi-branch networks that efficiently share features across tasks for real-time inference.

papers.title: Open-Set Gait Recognition from Sparse mmWave Radar Point Clouds
papers.summary: Performs open-set gait recognition from sparse radar point clouds by learning discriminative features that generalize to unseen identities.

papers.title: mmReID: Person Reidentification Based on Commodity Millimeter-Wave Radar
papers.summary: Enables person re-identification across camera views using commodity mmWave radar by extracting gait and body shape signatures.

papers.title: Through-Wall Cross-Domain User Identification via Lip Movement Micro-Doppler and MIMO Radar: an Unsupervised Domain Adaptation Approach
papers.summary: Achieves through-wall user identification by detecting lip movement micro-Doppler with MIMO radar and using domain adaptation across environments.

papers.title: Causal Localization Network for Radar Human Localization With Micro-Doppler Signature
papers.summary: Introduces the novel task of temporally localizing human identifications within untrimmed micro-Doppler signatures and proposes a baseline Causal Localization Network (CLNet) to solve it.

papers.title: Mission: mmWave Radar Person Identification with RGB Cameras
papers.summary: Proposes the first cross-modal ReID system that identifies a person in RGB images based on their detection by mmWave radar, using a novel similarity estimation to bridge the 3D-coarse (radar) to 2D-fine-grained (image) modal gap.

papers.title: Simultaneous Authentication of Multiple Users Using a Single mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Solves the challenge of simultaneous multi-user authentication by using a dynamically rotating radar to isolate and identify the unique, fine-grained breathing patterns of different individuals from blended RF signals.

papers.title: See-Through Soil: Underground Root Tuber Sensing With RF Sensor Networks
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
papers.summary: Proposes a deep learning framework (MC-Diffusion) that uses latent diffusion models and domain adaptation techniques to reconstruct fine-grained, cross-sectional images of root tubers from RF sensor network data.

papers.title: SPYCE: A Multi-Modal Rodent Monitoring Device for Enhanced Detection, Monitoring, and Behavior Analysis
papers.summary: Proposes an open-source, T-shaped multi-modal device (SPYCE) that integrates PIR, ultrasonic, camera, and radar sensors, using flexible activation logic to collect data for training deep-learning-based rodent behavior analysis models.

papers.title: Sem-RaDiff: Diffusion-Based 3D Radar Semantic Perception in Cluttered Agricultural Environments
papers.summary: Tackles radar perception in cluttered agricultural scenes by using a hierarchical diffusion model that first filters sidelobe artifacts and then generates a fine-grained 3D semantic point cloud, enabling the accurate reconstruction of thin structures.

papers.title: Hydra: Accurate Multi-Modal Leaf Wetness Sensing with mm-Wave and Camera Fusion
papers.summary: Achieves accurate leaf wetness sensing for precision agriculture by fusing mm-wave radar's dielectric sensing with camera's visual information.

papers.title: Omnidirectional Solid-State mmWave Radar Perception for UAV Power Line Collision Avoidance
papers.summary: Addresses UAV power line collision risks by proposing a lightweight, omnidirectional mmWave radar system.  It integrates multiple solid-state modules for spherical coverage, enabling reliable detection of wires as thin as 1.2mm at 10m distances and successful avoidance at speeds up to 10m/s.

papers.title: InspecSafe-V1: A Multimodal Benchmark for Safety Assessment in Industrial Inspection Scenarios
papers.summary: Introduces InspecSafe-V1, a comprehensive multimodal dataset (radar, RGB, thermal) for safety assessment in industrial inspection scenarios, along with benchmark models and evaluation protocols to advance research in this domain.

papers.title: RadarFuseNet: Complex-Valued Attention-Based Fusion of IQ Time- and Frequency-Domain Radar Features for Classification Tasks
papers.summary: Enhances mmWave radar classification robustness by proposing RadarFuseNet, a framework that employs a bidirectional cross-attention mechanism to fuse complex-valued time-domain (IQ) and frequency-domain (FFT) features, significantly improving accuracy for both material identification and occluded object recognition.

papers.title: ACCOR: Attention-Enhanced Complex-Valued Contrastive Learning for Occluded Object Classification Using mmWave Radar IQ Signals
papers.summary: Enhances occluded object classification in industrial settings by proposing ACCOR, a model that processes complex-valued mmWave radar IQ signals with an attention-enhanced CNN and a hybrid contrastive loss, outperforming existing methods at both 64 GHz and 67 GHz.

papers.title: CRFusion: Fine-Grained Object Identification Using RF-Image Modality Fusion
papers.summary: Enables fine-grained object identification in industrial settings by fusing RF material signatures with visual appearance for enhanced discrimination.

papers.title: Attacking mmWave Imaging With Neural Meta-Material Rendering
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
papers.summary: Demonstrates vulnerabilities in mmWave imaging systems by using neural meta-materials to create adversarial patterns that deceive radar-based detection.

papers.title: mmHSE: Enhanced Eavesdropping Attack on Headsets Leveraging COTS mmWave Radar
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Enhances a radar-based eavesdropping attack on headsets by combining a high-resolution beamformer (MVDR+CZT) for precise vibration capture, a spatiotemporal fitting method for signal isolation, and a GAN for audio refinement.

papers.title: SPACE: Speaker Adaptation for Acoustic Eavesdropping using mmWave Radio Signals
papers.summary: Tackles the challenge of speaker variability in mmWave eavesdropping by using a speaker encoder to extract and adapt to individual speaker characteristics, enabling more accurate speech reconstruction.

papers.title: Acoustic Eavesdropping From Sound-Induced Vibrations With Multi-Antenna mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Overcomes the need for prior training data in acoustic eavesdropping by using multi-antenna signal processing to achieve micrometer-level vibration extraction for speech recovery.

papers.title: EM-Aware Physical Synthesis: Neural Inductor Modeling and Intelligent Placement & Routing for RF Circuits
papers.summary: Addresses the inability of current machine learning approaches to produce manufacturable RF circuit layouts by proposing an ML-driven framework for automated RF physical synthesis.  This framework transforms circuit netlists into manufacturable GDSII layouts through three key innovations: a highly accurate neural inductor model trained across 1-100 GHz, an intelligent P-Cell optimizer for area reduction, and a complete placement and routing engine that enforces frequency-dependent electromagnetic (EM) spacing and Design Rule Check (DRC) compliance.

papers.title: Cognitive FMCW Automotive Radar for Interference Suppression
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
papers.summary: Addresses mutual interference in dense traffic by proposing a cognitive FMCW radar framework that uses silent chirps and Fractional Fourier Transform (FrFT) to sense the environment. It adaptively adjusts chirp rates to mitigate coherent interference and employs a two-stage iterative strategy (orthogonal projection and soft-thresholding) to suppress long-duration non-coherent interference.

papers.title: Towards Radar-Driven Speech Therapy: Multimodal Training with Ultrasound, Audio, and Radar for Unimodal Phonetic Segment Classification
papers.summary: Advances radar-based speech therapy by integrating ultrasound, audio, and radar data to train models for phonetic segment classification, enabling unimodal radar-based assessment of speech articulation.

papers.title: Radar and OpenStreetMap-Aided Consistent Trajectory Estimation in Canopy-Occluded Environments
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of localization in GNSS-denied, canopy-occluded environments by presenting a radar-based coarse-to-fine trajectory estimation framework that integrates temporally enhanced features with adaptive OpenStreetMap priors, reducing Absolute Trajectory Error by up to 55.23%.

papers.title: MULTIAQUA: A multimodal maritime dataset and robust training strategies for multimodal semantic segmentation
papers.summary: Addresses the limitations of vision-only maritime perception in challenging conditions by introducing MULTIAQUA, a synchronized multimodal dataset (RGB, thermal, IR, LiDAR, Radar), and proposing robust training strategies that allow models to learn from daytime data while maintaining high performance in near-complete darkness.

papers.title: CF-Net: A Cross-Feature Reconstruction Network for High-Accuracy 1-Bit Target Classification
papers.summary: Overcomes the significant information loss in 1-bit radar quantization by proposing CF-Net, a two-stage framework that employs a self-supervised cross-feature reconstruction strategy to recover high-fidelity features, enabling high-accuracy target classification without the need for impractical oversampling.

papers.title: A Multi-Robot Platform for Robotic Triage Combining Onboard Sensing and Foundation Models
papers.summary: Automates the complete, high-risk primary triage process in mass-casualty incidents by deploying a heterogeneous air-ground robotic team (UAVs/UGVs) that leverages onboard sensing and foundation models to perform victim localization, vital sign measurement, and injury assessment.

papers.title: VP-AutoTest: A Virtual-Physical Fusion Autonomous Driving Testing Platform
papers.summary: Overcomes the limitations of traditional simulations and closed-course testing by proposing a comprehensive virtual-physical fusion platform (VP-AutoTest) that integrates diverse mixed-reality elements, supports multi-vehicle cooperation, and utilizes adversarial strategies along with credibility self-evaluation to ensure high-fidelity testing.

papers.title: mmMUSE: An mmWave-based Motion-resilient Universal Speech Enhancement System
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Addresses the challenge of motion-induced signal dispersion in mmWave speech enhancement by employing a two-stage vocal extraction method and a complex-valued fusion network to robustly integrate audio and radar data.

papers.title: AI4X Roadmap: Artificial Intelligence for the advancement of scientific pursuit and its future directions
papers.summary: Presents a comprehensive roadmap for the integration of AI in scientific research, outlining key areas of impact, challenges, and future directions to guide researchers and policymakers.

papers.title: BeamCKM: A Framework of Channel Knowledge Map Construction for Multi-Antenna Systems
papers.summary: Addresses the incompatibility between traditional pathloss maps and multi-antenna beamforming by proposing a "BeamCKM" concept and a transformer-based architecture (CKMTransUNet/M3ChanNet) that leverages multi-modal environmental data to construct high-fidelity channel knowledge maps.

papers.title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Wireless Channel Estimation with Limited Pilot Signals
papers.publication: Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
papers.summary: ackles the challenge of channel estimation with limited pilots by employing a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) that fuses initial estimates with environmental RSS maps through a Transformer-enhanced U-Net to achieve high accuracy and interpretability.

papers.title: We Can Hear You with mmWave Radar! An End-to-End Eavesdropping System
papers.summary: Achieves through-wall speech eavesdropping by using a mmWave radar to capture loudspeaker-induced vibrations and a deep neural network to reconstruct intelligible speech from the resulting noisy spectrograms.

papers.title: Multimodal-Wireless: A Large-Scale Dataset for Sensing and Communication
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of datasets for joint sensing and communication research by releasing a large-scale, simulated dataset (Multimodal-Wireless) that uses a CARLA/Sionna pipeline to generate communication channel data synchronized with standard sensor modalities.

papers.title: RASE 2026: Radar Acoustic Speech Enhancement
papers.summary: Enhancement of Speech Signals Acquired Through Glass Using mmWave Radar

papers.title: Micro-Doppler Classification of Humans and Animals Using FMCW Radar
papers.summary: Uses FMCW radar micro-Doppler spectrograms with a two-stage SVM classifier to first differentiate humans from animals and then identify specific animal species for anti-poaching.

papers.title: A Comprehensive Radar-Based Berthing-Aid Dataset (R-BAD) and Onboard System for Safe Vessel Docking
papers.summary: Addresses the lack of public data for autonomous ship berthing by introducing a large-scale radar and video dataset (R-BAD) and a corresponding onboard system to enable the development of all-weather docking solutions.

papers.title: How to Talk to Your Classifier: Conditional Text Generation with Radar–Visual Latent Space
papers.summary: Addresses the interpretability of radar classifiers by training a model to simultaneously classify an image and generate descriptive text from its latent space, using the text to reveal how the classifier interprets the visual data.

papers.title: Radar Can See and Hear as Well: A New Multimodal Benchmark Based on Radar Sensing
papers.summary: Introduces the RACER dataset, a multimodal benchmark with synchronized radar, audio, and visual data, to evaluate radar's ability to sense speech by detecting vocal cord and lip movements.

papers.title: Wavoice: An mmWave-Assisted Noise-Resistant Speech Recognition System
papers.summary: Enhances speech recognition in noisy environments by using mmWave radar to capture lip and vocal cord movements, fusing this data with audio inputs for improved accuracy.

papers.title: Seeing the Invisible: Recovering Surveillance Video With COTS mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Solves the problem of camera failure/obscuration by using a generative network to fuse real-time mmWave motion data with pre-acquired visual appearance data to reconstruct the video.

papers.title: UFace: Your Smartphone Can "Hear" Your Facial Expression!
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Solves the challenge of unobtrusive facial expression recognition by using a smartphone's acoustic signals (not camera/radar) with a dual-stream attention network to extract fine-grained motion-related changes.

papers.title: Introducing an indoor object classification dataset including sparse point clouds from mmWave radar
papers.summary: Fills the gap in public datasets for indoor object classification by releasing RadIOCD, which contains synchronized sparse mmWave radar point clouds and RGB-D images of common household items.

papers.title: SuperSight: Sub-cm NLOS Localization for mmWave Backscatter
papers.publication: ACM International Conference on Mobile Systems, Applications, and Services (MobiSys)
papers.summary: Achieves sub-cm non-line-of-sight (NLOS) localization by using a triangular retro-reflective tag array to uniquely exploit directional mmWave multipath, eliminating the need for environmental profiling.

papers.title: WiCompass: Oracle-driven Data Scaling for mmWave Human Pose Estimation
papers.summary: Improves out-of-distribution mmWave human pose estimation by introducing WiCompass, a coverage-aware data collection framework that builds a universal pose-space oracle from large-scale motion-capture corpora and prioritizes underrepresented motions for more efficient and robust data scaling.

papers.title: RADE-Net: Robust Attention Network for Radar-Only Object Detection in Adverse Weather
papers.summary: Proposes RADE-Net, a lightweight radar-only 3D object detector that projects RADE tensors into a Doppler- and elevation-preserving 3D representation, cutting per-frame data size by 91.9% while improving K-Radar performance, especially in adverse weather.

papers.title: m^3TrackFormer: Transformer-based mmWave Multi-Target Tracking with Lost Target Re-Acquisition Capability
papers.summary: Presents m^3TrackFormer, a Transformer-based mmWave multi-target tracking framework for ISAC systems that switches between normal tracking and re-acquisition modes to recover lost targets quickly with low beam-sweeping overhead.

papers.title: HP-C4D: A Fast Camera and 4D Radar Fusion Framework with Height Prediction For 3D Object Detection
papers.summary: Introduces HP-C4D, a fast camera and 4D radar fusion framework with Height BEVPool, PVHG, and IGF to improve 3D object detection accuracy and height estimation while retaining real-time speed.

papers.title: A Lightweight Adaptive Camera-Radar Fusion for Object Detection Under Fog-Haze Environment
papers.summary: Improves adverse-weather obstacle detection by combining lightweight camera-radar fusion with gated attention and dynamic defogging to better detect small and distant objects in fog-haze scenes.

papers.title: AceNet: Attention-Guided Context Enhancement for Imbalanced Action Recognition via RF Signals
papers.summary: Addresses imbalanced RF-based action recognition by combining attention-guided segment selection, hierarchical Transformer context modeling, and feature-space SMOTE refinement to separate semantically similar activities.

papers.title: One-Legged Stand Test: Synchronized Motion Capture, Force Plate, and Radar Dataset for Fall-Risk
papers.summary: Releases a synchronized one-legged-stand dataset with motion capture, force plates, 24 GHz FMCW radar, and event labels to support balance analysis and fall-risk assessment.

papers.title: Boosting Instance Awareness via Cross-View Correlation with 4D Radar and Camera for 3D Object Detection
papers.summary: Improves 3D radar-camera detection by injecting 2D instance cues into BEV through cross-view correlation and scene-instance aware Transformer fusion, boosting robustness under weak radar geometry.

papers.title: SD4R: Sparse-to-Dense Learning for 3D Object Detection with 4D Radar
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITSC)
papers.summary: Transforms sparse 4D radar point clouds into dense representations with foreground point generation and logit-query encoding to improve robust 3D object detection.

papers.title: LGDD: Local-Global Synergistic Dual-Branch 3D Object Detection Using 4D Radar
papers.publication: IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
papers.summary: Addresses sparse and noisy 4D radar point clouds with a local-global dual-branch detector that combines voxel-attended point features, query-based pre-fusion, and semantics-geometry aware fusion for stronger 3D object detection.

papers.title: RFInv: Uncovering Sensitive Data in RF Sensing Systems via Model Inversion
papers.summary: Addresses privacy leakage in RF sensing by proposing the first black-box model inversion attack that reconstructs sensitive RF data from classifier outputs for identity impersonation and unauthorized device control.

papers.title: Autoregressive Models for Fast Breathing Rate Estimation With Distributed Elevated Radars
papers.summary: Improves contactless breathing-rate estimation by combining automatic range-bin selection with fast autoregressive spectral analysis for ceiling-mounted distributed radars in clinically safer, long-distance monitoring setups.

papers.title: Multi-modal Large Language Models for Perception in Autonomous Driving: Architecture, Taxonomy, and Challenges
papers.summary: Surveys multimodal large language models for autonomous-driving perception by organizing tasks, datasets, architectures, alignment strategies, and scene representations for language-enhanced multi-sensor reasoning.

papers.title: MKFusion: Multi-modal knowledge distillation for 4D radar point cloud segmentation in autonomous driving
papers.summary: Improves 4D radar point cloud segmentation by combining radar-camera fusion and knowledge distillation to learn sparse multi-modal representations for autonomous driving.

papers.title: How IMU Drift Influences Multi-Radar Inertial Odometry for Ground Robots in Subterranean Terrains
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Improves subterranean radar-inertial odometry by using a two-stage framework that estimates IMU bias from radar ego-velocity and fuses multi-radar and IMU data for resilient localization and mapping.

papers.title: Robust mmWave Radar Sensing with Multi-sensor Temporal Calibration and Supervision
papers.summary: Improves low-cost mmWave radar sensing by combining visual-inertial supervision, dynamic 3D reconstruction, spurious-point filtering, and temporal calibration for detection, localization, and mapping.

papers.title: mmWave Radar Aware Dual-Conditioned GAN for Speech Reconstruction of Signals With Low SNR
papers.summary: Reconstructs intelligible speech from low-SNR mmWave radar signals through glass using a dual-conditioned GAN with multi-mel discrimination and residual fusion of conditioning channels.

papers.title: physfusion: A Transformer-based Dual-Stream Radar and Vision Fusion Framework for Open Water Surface Object Detection
papers.summary: Addresses water-surface object detection by fusing sparse 4D radar and vision through a physics-informed dual-stream Transformer with reliability-aware radar encoding and temporal query aggregation for robust maritime perception.

papers.title: MSET: Multimodal Semantic-Enhanced Real-World Beam Prediction via Temporal Modeling with Visual Foundation Models
papers.summary: Improves real-world beam prediction by combining visual foundation model semantics, causal temporal modeling, and GPS-conditioned cross-attention to reduce sweep reliance and improve robustness in V2I scenarios.

papers.title: Analysis of Proactive Uncoordinated Techniques to Mitigate Interference in FMCW Automotive Radars
papers.summary: Analyzes uncoordinated FMCW radar interference mitigation by modeling correlated interference in realistic traffic and showing chirp-by-chirp frequency hopping with sufficient bandwidth best preserves automotive radar reliability.

papers.title: LITE: Local Isometry-Invariant Topology Encoding for Efficient 3-D Sensing With 4-D Radar Point Clouds
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.summary: Improves 3D radar object detection by encoding local isometry-invariant topology, velocity, and reflectivity cues in sparse 4D radar point clouds for accurate and efficient standalone sensing.

papers.title: Control-Assisted Beam Prediction and Tracking for UAV Millimeter Wave Communications
papers.summary: Improves UAV mmWave beam alignment by exploiting control-theoretic flight properties in a learning-based beam prediction and tracking framework that reduces complexity for real-world mission flights.

papers.title: CFEAR-Teach-and-Repeat: Fast and Accurate Radar-only Localization
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Achieves radar-only teach-and-repeat localization by jointly aligning live spinning-radar scans to teach-pass maps and recent keyframes for fast, accurate, and weather-robust navigation.

papers.title: RLPR: Radar-to-LiDAR Place Recognition via Two-Stage Asymmetric Cross-Modal Alignment for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Addresses radar-to-LiDAR place recognition by using dual-stream structural feature learning and two-stage asymmetric cross-modal alignment for robust all-weather localization across diverse radar types.

papers.title: calibfusion: Transformer-Based Differentiable Calibration for Radar-Camera Fusion Detection in Water-Surface Environments
papers.summary: Improves water-surface radar-camera detection by learning differentiable extrinsic refinement and calibration-conditioned radar projection for robust fusion under misalignment and clutter.

papers.title: DRIFT: Dual-Representation Inter-Fusion Transformer for Automated Driving Perception with 4D Radar Point Clouds
papers.summary: Improves radar-only automated driving perception by fusing local point features and global pillar context through a dual-path Transformer that boosts 4D radar object detection and free-road estimation on VoD and internal data.

papers.title: A dataset of 120 GHz millimeter-wave radar vital signals with synchronized reference recordings
papers.summary: Releases a 120 GHz FMCW radar vital-sign dataset with synchronized ECG, respiration, pulse waveform, and blood-pressure references from 24 subjects to support biomedical radar signal-processing research.

papers.title: STONE Dataset: A Scalable Multi-Modal Surround-View 3D Traversability Dataset for Off-Road Robot Navigation
papers.publication: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
papers.summary: Introduces STONE, a large-scale off-road robot-navigation dataset with surround-view RGB, LiDAR, and 4D radar sensing plus annotation-free geometry-aware 3D traversability labels for benchmarking voxel-level perception.

papers.title: RCOC-Distill: Boosting 4D Radar-Camera Online Calibration with Knowledge Distillation from LiDAR Features
papers.summary: Improves 4D radar-camera online calibration by distilling LiDAR-informed cross-modal features into sparse radar representations through local correlation and radar-LiDAR alignment modules for more accurate extrinsic correction.
