papers.title: Millimeter-wave radar heart rate monitoring via time-frequency fusion and composite filtering with ECG alignment
papers.summary: Improves non-contact heart-rate monitoring by combining composite filtering, drift compensation, ECG alignment, FFT, and cepstrum analysis to stabilize mmWave radar estimation under noise and respiratory interference.

papers.title: AdaRadar: Rate Adaptive Spectral Compression for Radar-based Perception
papers.summary: Radar is a critical perception modality in autonomous driving systems due to its all-weather characteristics and ability to measure range and Doppler velocity.

papers.title: LRC-WeatherNet: LiDAR, RADAR, and Camera Fusion Network for Real-time Weather-type Classification in Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Autonomous vehicles face major perception and navigation challenges in adverse weather such as rain, fog, and snow, which degrade the performance of LiDAR, RADAR, and RGB camera sensors.

papers.title: Radar-Inertial Odometry with Online Spatio-Temporal Calibration via Continuous-Time IMU Modeling
papers.summary: Radar-Inertial Odometry (RIO) has emerged as a robust alternative to vision- and LiDAR-based odometry in challenging conditions such as low light, fog, featureless environments, or in adverse weather.

papers.title: A Cross Gait Dual-Stream Network with Multi-Period Modeling for Gait Recognition Based on Millimeter-Wave Radar Signals
papers.summary: Addresses radar-based gait recognition by using a dual-stream network with multi-period modeling to capture complementary temporal patterns from millimeter-wave radar signals for identity-aware recognition.

papers.title: RadarGaussianDet3D: Gaussian Representation-Based Real-Time 3D Object Detection With 4D Automotive Radars
papers.summary: Improves real-time 3D object detection by representing 4D automotive radar observations with Gaussians, enabling fast and accurate detection for autonomous driving.

papers.title: Sensing Life in Stillness: Unified Dynamic and Static Human Mesh Reconstruction with mmWave Radar
papers.publication: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
papers.summary: Addresses dynamic and static human mesh reconstruction for rehabilitation by combining micro-motion feature extraction and geometry-aware knowledge transfer with mmWave radar.

papers.title: DriveEmo-FL: in-cabin radar-based emotion sensing for autonomous vehicles smart response
papers.summary: Addresses in-cabin emotion sensing by combining micro-Doppler and velocity-time features with federated learning for privacy-preserving radar-based affect recognition in autonomous vehicles.

papers.title: RadarXFormer: Robust Object Detection via Cross-Dimension Fusion of 4D Radar Spectra and Images for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Improves robust object detection by cross-dimension fusion of 4D radar spectra and camera images for autonomous driving under challenging conditions.

papers.title: CLRNet: Targetless Extrinsic Calibration for Camera, Lidar and 4D Radar Using Deep Learning
papers.summary: Addresses targetless extrinsic calibration by using deep learning to jointly calibrate camera, LiDAR, and 4D radar sensors without dedicated calibration targets.

papers.title: Beam Prediction Based on Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.summary: Addresses proactive beam prediction by using a multimodal large language model that fuses camera and LiDAR context for robust beam selection in dynamic wireless environments.

papers.title: Towards Accurate 3D Object Detection in Adverse Weather by Leveraging 4D Radar for LiDAR Geometry Enhancement
papers.publication: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence
papers.summary: Improves adverse-weather 3D object detection by using 4D radar to enhance LiDAR geometry for more reliable autonomous-driving perception under degraded visibility.

papers.title: Resilient Multi-Radar Inertial Odometry with Adaptive Bias Correction for Localization in Smoke-Filled Subterranean Environments
papers.summary: Addresses subterranean localization by correcting IMU bias and fusing multiple mmWave radars with inertial data for resilient radar-inertial odometry and mapping in smoke-filled environments.

papers.title: mmJEPA-ECG: Cross-Posture Robust Contactless Electrocardiogram Monitoring via Millimeter Wave Radar Sensing
papers.publication: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence
papers.summary: Addresses contactless electrocardiogram monitoring by learning cross-posture radar representations with a JEPA-based framework for robust millimeter-wave ECG sensing.

papers.title: mmWave-Diffusion:A Novel Framework for Respiration Sensing Using Observation-Anchored Conditional Diffusion Model
papers.summary: Improves contactless respiration sensing by using an observation-anchored conditional diffusion model to remove micromotion interference from mmWave radar phase measurements.

papers.title: AW-MoE: All-Weather Mixture of Experts for Robust Multi-Modal 3D Object Detection
papers.summary: Improves all-weather multi-modal 3D object detection by routing weather-specific features through a mixture-of-experts design for robust autonomous-driving perception.

papers.title: Physically Accurate Differentiable Inverse Rendering for Radio Frequency Digital Twin
papers.summary: Addresses radio frequency digital twin construction by introducing differentiable inverse rendering for physically accurate RF scene parameter estimation.

papers.title: Tightly-Coupled Radar-Visual-Inertial Odometry
papers.summary: Improves state estimation by tightly coupling radar, vision, and inertial sensing for robust odometry across dark, low-texture, and obscured environments.

papers.title: mmAnomaly: Leveraging Visual Context for Robust Anomaly Detection in the Non-Visual World with mmWave Radar
papers.publication: the 24th ACM/IEEE International Conference on Embedded Artificial Intelligence and Sensing Systems (SenSys 2026)
papers.summary: mmWave radar enables human sensing in non-visual scenarios-e.g., through clothing or certain types of walls-where traditional cameras fail due to occlusion or privacy limitations.

papers.title: Body Area Networks
papers.summary: This two-volume set LNICST 666-667 constitutes the refereed proceedings of the19th EAI International Conference on Body Area Networks, BODYNETS 2024, held in Varanasi, India, during December 15–16, 2024.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion using Hybrid Attention for Autonomous Driving
papers.summary: Accurate 3D object detection for autonomous driving requires complementary sensors.

papers.title: Foundation Models Defining A New Era In Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition: A Survey And Outlook
papers.summary: Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition (HAR) underpins many ubiquitous and wearable computing applications, yet current models remain limited by scarce labels, sensor heterogeneity, and weak generalization across users, devices, and contexts.

papers.title: RAVEN: Radar Adaptive Vision Encoders for Efficient Chirp-wise Object Detection and Segmentation
papers.publication: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Conference 2026
papers.summary: This paper presents RAVEN, a computationally efficient deep learning architecture for FMCW radar perception.

papers.title: DSERT-RoLL: Robust Multi-Modal Perception for Diverse Driving Conditions with Stereo Event-RGB-Thermal Cameras, 4D Radar, and Dual-LiDAR
papers.summary: In this paper, we present DSERT-RoLL, a driving dataset that incorporates stereo event, RGB, and thermal cameras together with 4D radar and dual LiDAR, collected across diverse weather and illumination conditions.

papers.title: Geometrically-Constrained Radar-Inertial Odometry via Continuous Point-Pose Uncertainty Modeling
papers.summary: Radar odometry is crucial for robust localization in challenging environments; however, the sparsity of reliable returns and distinctive noise characteristics impede its performance.

papers.title: Multi-Sensor Datasets of Ultrasonic and mmWave Reflected Signals for Material Classification
papers.summary: Metadata could not be resolved automatically; review the source paper before importing: Multi-Sensor Datasets of Ultrasonic and mmWave Reflected Signals for Material Classification.

papers.title: Purify-then-Align: Towards Robust Human Sensing under Modality Missing with Knowledge Distillation from Noisy Multimodal Teacher
papers.summary: Robust multimodal human sensing must overcome the critical challenge of missing modalities.

papers.title: Quasi-stationary Slice Detection-Based Robust Respiration Rate Estimation under Large-scale Random Body Movement
papers.summary: Radar-based non-contact respiration rate (RR) measurement has become increasingly popular due to its convenience, non-intrusiveness, and low cost.

papers.title: Non-Contact Heart Rate Detection Using FMCW Radar Based on 1-D Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.summary: Proposes a 1-D CNN approach for non-contact heart rate detection using FMCW radar signals.

papers.title: From Bits to Tokens: Knowledge-Driven Generative Communication of Multimodal Data
papers.summary: Proposes a knowledge-driven generative communication framework that tokenizes multimodal sensing data for efficient semantic transmission.

papers.title: RF-LEGO: Modularized Signal Processing-Deep Learning Co-Design for RF Sensing via Deep Unrolling
papers.summary: Co-designs modular signal-processing and deep-learning blocks for RF sensing via deep unrolling.

papers.title: FeelWave: Enabling Emotion-Aware Voice Interaction through Noise-Robust mmWave Emotion Sensing
papers.summary: Enables emotion-aware voice interaction by using mmWave sensing to infer emotion robustly under acoustic noise.

papers.title: FlowGait: Enabling Robust Long-Term Gait Recognition Across Real-World Covariates with mmWave Radar
papers.summary: Addresses long-term mmWave radar gait recognition across real-world covariates for robust identity recognition.

papers.title: SynFlow: Scaling Up LiDAR Scene Flow Estimation with Synthetic Data
papers.summary: Reliable 3D dynamic perception requires models that can anticipate motion beyond predefined categories, yet progress is hindered by the scarcity of dense, high-quality motion annotations.

papers.title: "Take Me Home, Wi-Fi Drone": A Drone-based Wireless System for Wilderness Search and Rescue
papers.publication: In The 32nd Annual International Conference on Mobile Computing and Networking (MobiCom '26)
papers.summary: Wilderness Search and Rescue (WiSAR) represents a longstanding and critical societal challenge, demanding innovative and automatic technological solutions.
